question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 42144/22-27, whether (i) his Department; (ii) Health and Social Care Trusts; and (iii) any of his Department's arm's-length bodies, engaged with (a) Stonewall; (b) the Rainbow Project; and (c) any other LGBT+ organisation, on the subject of self-identified gender fields within Health and Social Care systems.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) Translink's policy regarding the provision of printed timetables at railway stations, including City Hospital station; and (ii) any alternative arrangements in place to ensure passengers without access to mobile devices can obtain accurate and up-to-date service information.
To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 42144/22-27, whether it is departmental policy that biological sex will be recorded in all Health and Social Care systems as a mandatory field.

To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 42144/22-27, to detail (i) when the introduction of self-identified gender fields within Health and Social Care systems was authorised; and (ii) who authorised this.

To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he will commission a Job Evaluation and Grading Support review of all posts, including the Grade 7 Head of Science, within the science team of the Loughs Agency.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to several Assembly Questions in relation to the Loughs Agency acting outside its staff complement, to detail the cost, in each year since 2021, of employing additional staff without approval, including (i) salary; (ii) ERNIC payments; (iii) employers pension contributions; (iv) office furniture; (v) mobile telephone; (vi) IT equipment; and (vii) personal protective equipment.

To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the security vetting requirements that apply to Special Advisers appointed in her Department prior to appointment.

To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the security vetting requirements that apply to Special Advisers appointed in her Department prior to appointment.

To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any work undertaken within her Department in light of the UK Government’s expansion of nuclear power and the development of Small Modular Reactor technology.
To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the security vetting requirements that apply to Special Advisers prior to appointment.
To ask the Minister for Communities whether he is currently considering options from the Housing Executive on how to raise revenue within the Executive.
To ask the Minister for Communities whether he has any plans to review the Right to Buy scheme, which was last reviewed in 2018.

To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement she, or her officials, have had with the (i) Minister of State for Science, Innovation, Research and Nuclear; and (ii) Department for Energy Security and Net Zero, in relation to nuclear energy in Northern Ireland in the last 12 months.
To ask the Minister for Communities whether the inclusion of bungalows in the Right to Buy scheme is currently under consideration.
answer_text: I have no plans to consider the inclusion of bungalows within the Housing Executive’s Statutory House Sales Scheme. Bungalows were removed from the Scheme several years ago to protect the availability of this relatively scarce form of accommodation within the social housing stock. This decision was taken in recognition of the growing older population and the importance of retaining suitable housing options that support independent living.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the nature of the meeting held with the Assistant Chief Constable in Parliament Buildings on Monday 13 April 2026, including whether fuel protests were discussed.
answer_text: I did not have a meeting with an Assistant Chief Constable on Monday 13th April 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he has had any discussions with Executive colleagues in relation to introducing a scheme similar to the holiday activities and food programme in England.
answer_text: My officials’ latest projections are that the Finance Minister’s draft three-year budget would result in a shortfall to my Department estimated to be around £600 million in 2026/27, rising to £800 million by 2028/29. These unfunded pressures are prohibitive to the introduction of similar schemes such as the Holiday activities and Food Programme currently running in England.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether any assessment has been made to ascertain whether all Barnett consequentials received by the Northern Ireland Executive relating to the holiday activities and food programme end up with his Department.
To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the Barnett consequentials received by the Northern Ireland Executive relating to the holiday activities and food programme.

To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) any assessment that has been made of the adequacy of physiotherapist numbers within the Health Service; and (ii) any measures being taken to address any shortfall.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail how the number of physiotherapists per head of population in Northern Ireland compares with the figure in Great Britain.
answer_text: Physiotherapists are regulated by the Health and Care Professions Council (HCPC).  By law, anyone wishing to practice physiotherapy in the UK must be registered with the HCPC.  A breakdown of HCPC registered physiotherapists in each UK region, at January 2026, is detailed in the table below.  Mid-year population estimates and the number of registered physiotherapists per 100,000 population in each UK region is also detailed in the table below.HCPC Registered Physiotherapists per 100,000 Population, by UK RegionUK RegionHCPC Registered Physiotherapists *Population Estimates #Physiotherapists per 100,000 populationNorthern Ireland2,4201,927,900126England60,36558,620,100103Scotland5,6205,546,900101Wales2,8753,186,60090Source: Information & Analysis Directorate, DoH* Figures as at January 2026, obtained from HCPC at: https://www.hcpc-uk.org/data/the-register/register-over-time-by-characteristics-and-location/# Based on 2024 mid-year population estimates, obtained from the Office for National Statistics (ONS) at: https://www.ons.gov.uk/peoplepopulationandcommunity/populationandmigration/populationestimates

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the total number of drivers issued with penalty points for driving too slowly in each of the last three years, broken down by geographical area.
answer_text: The Department does not hold the information requested.  The recording of information on persons on number of drivers / pedestrians detected for a motoring offence is a matter for the Chief Constable.  You may, therefore, wish to direct any further query relating to the number of drivers issued with penalty points for driving too slowly to the PSNI.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether any of the information withheld in a recent FOI request from the News Letter relating to the Cass review relates to concerns about clinical practice, patient outcomes or the long-term impact of gender interventions.

To ask the Minister of Health to detail the criteria used by his Department to determine whether information in the Cass Review report should be withheld or redacted in a recent FOI request, including whether reputational, political or stakeholder considerations were taken into account.

To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to several previous Assembly Written Questions on the Loughs Agency, to detail the cost of employing additional staff without approval in each year since 2021, including (i) salary; (ii) ERNIC payments; (iii) employers' pension contributions; (iv) office furniture; (v) mobile telephone costs; (vi) IT equipment; and (vii) personal protective equipment.
answer_text: It has not been identified that the Loughs Agency employed additional staff without approval in any year since 2021.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the number of deaths by suicide in each of the last three years, broken down by (i) age; (ii) gender; and (iii) local council area.
answer_text: NISRA produces official statistics on causes of death using registration data collected by the General Register Office (GRO).The number of deaths by suicide, in each of the last three years for which statistics are currently available, can be found in the Suicide Deaths 2024 Report. The breakdowns requested can be found in Table 2 (age and sex) and Table 6 (local government district).

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any measures being taken by his Department to address the issue of suicide, particularly as it relates to young men.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of physiotherapists employed in each hospital on (i) 1 April 2026; and (ii) 1 April 2016.
answer_text: Information on the whole-time equivalent (WTE) of staff employed in the Health and Social Care (HSC) Trusts is published by my Department on a quarterly basis.  The latest information available relates to the position at 31 December 2025 and can be found at the link below.https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/publications/northern-ireland-health-and-social-care-hsc-workforce-statistics-december-2025Physiotherapists work in different settings across HSC Trusts, in both hospital and community locations. question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether any assessment has been carried out on the short supply of anti-seizure medicine Zonegran 50mg in Northern Ireland and his Department's plan to remedy this as a matter of urgency.
answer_text: The Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC) leads on the maintenance of medicine supply chains to the UK and marketing authorisation holders have a legal obligation to inform DHSC of any anticipated shortages or discontinuations across the UK within six months or as soon as reasonably practicable. My Department works closely with DHSC and others across the UK to ensure all UK patients have equitable access to the treatments they require by applying well-established tools and processes if a medicine shortage does occur. My officials were not aware of supply disruptions affecting Zonegran® 50mg capsules until a concern was raised with my Department on 13 April 2026, but I am pleased to confirm that Zonegran® 50mg capsules are currently available to order from a local pharmaceutical wholesaler. Therefore, no assessments or shortage management plans are required at this time.

To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the timeline on the demolition of the former Northern Regional College building on Trostan Avenue, Ballymena, given the prevalence of regular anti-social behaviour within the site including trespass, window smashing and fire lighting.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether the First Minister attended the 2026 commemoration of the Easter Rising in Dublin in her capacity as First Minister.

To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the sign-off process before a tweet marking the First Minister's attendance at the 2026 commemoration of the Easter Rising in Dublin was posted on the Northern Ireland Executive X account, including any agreement on the part of the deputy First Minister.

To ask the Assembly Commission, pursuant to AQW 40728/22-27, and in relation to the installation of the fully glazed partition in the Communications Office in Parliament Buildings, to detail (i) the dimensions and full specification of the partition, including the type and thickness of glass used; (ii) the number and value of quotations obtained; (iii) the procurement route used and the basis on which the successful supplier was selected; and (iv) the total cost of all associated refurbishment works, including any expenditure beyond the estimated £8,000 (excluding VAT).
To ask the Assembly Commission, pursuant to AQW 40728/22-27, to place a copy of the business case for this work in the Assembly library.

To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he will commission a Job Evaluation and Grading Support review of all posts within the aquaculture function of the Loughs Agency.
answer_text: I have no plans to commission a Job Evaluation and Grading Support review of all posts within the aquaculture function of Loughs Agency.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any defects, maintenance issues or seaworthiness concerns that were recorded in respect of the vessel Ostrea between 1 July 2015 and 31 December 2015, including any advice received from external contractors and any steps taken to address issues.
answer_text: Loughs Agency has confirmed that any relevant records relating to defects, maintenance issues or seaworthiness concerns in respect of the vessel Ostrea between 1 July 2015 and 31 December 2015 are no longer held. This is in accordance with its records management and retention policies.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail (i) whether any deficiencies have been identified in Schedule 3 to the Justice Act 2011 in respect of the regulation of NIFL matches; and (ii) whether her Department is considering any legislative amendment in relation to this.
answer_text: This matter is now the subject of live judicial review proceedings so I am unable to comment on the substance of this case.  Officials in my Department and the Department for Communities are working constructively in response.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the journeys for each Northern Health and Social Care Trust ambulance between 21:30 and 22:30 on 11 December 2025.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Ambulance Service (NIAS) is a regional service providing emergency, urgent and primary care services throughout the whole of Northern Ireland. There are 33 stations spread over 5,345 square miles to serve a population of almost 1.9 million. These stations are arranged into five operational divisions (Belfast, North, South, East and West) which align with the five Health and Social Care Trust boundaries.  NIAS divisions work collectively to provide appropriate, high-quality response to meet the clinical needs of the population of Northern Ireland.The number of emergency ambulances operational across Northern Ireland varies for the day of week and time of day.Table 1 shows the number of emergency ambulance crews that were on shift in the Northern HSC Trust area between 21:30 and 22:30 on 11 December 2025, broken down by the station at which they were based.  There were 23 personnel rostered over that date and time period, which equates to 11.5 crews.  Where a single member of staff is deployed for the ambulance service, they may be crewed with a colleague from another station or area in order to form a full operational crew.Table 1StationNo of PeopleNo of CrewAntrim21Ballycastle10.5Ballymena52.5Ballymoney21Carrickfergus10.5Coleraine10.5Cookstown21Larne21Magherafelt42Whiteabbey31.5Total2311.5Table 2 shows all calls where a vehicle was allocated and actively involved with the patient(s) located within the Northern HSC Trust area between 21:30 to 22:30 on 11 December.  This includes two ambulances from the Belfast division who responded to calls in the Northern division.  The data is broken down by Council Area.Table 2Local Council AreaNo. of CallsAntrim and Newtownabbey4Causeway Coast and Glens3Mid and East Antrim2Mid Ulster3Total12Table 3 shows the breakdown of the status of Northern division vehicles operating within the Northern Trust area, between 21:30 and 22:30 on 11 December 2025.Table 3JOURNEY TYPENO OF JOURNEYArrived at ED2Arrived at scene1Conveying to ED1Mobile to a call3To a call but stood down3Total10Table 4 shows all emergency calls that commenced between 21:30 and 22:30 on the 11th of December 2025.  These are broken down by local council area and category of call.Table 4Local Council AreaCategory 1Category 2Category 3Grand TotalAntrim and Newtownabbey 1 1Causeway Coast and Glens  11Mid and East Antrim 2 2Mid Ulster12 3Total1517

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail all communications between his Department, the Education Authority and the Department of Health in relation to healthcare provision for special schools’ summer schemes since January 2026.
answer_text: Senior officials in my Department, the EA and the Department of Health have engaged extensively through meetings and written communications, discussing healthcare staffing, oversight, and training needs. DoH confirmed in writing on Tuesday 24 March that they were unable to provide solutions as required. Since the original decision DoH has confirmed on-site nursing cover for all summer schemes in special schools, which reduces the healthcare risks making the schemes safe to run.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether any contingency planning was undertaken to secure temporary or agency healthcare staff to facilitate the special schools' summer schemes in 2026.
To ask the Minister of Education to detail the date he was first informed that special schools’ summer schemes for 2026 were at risk of cancellation.
answer_text: Officials advised me on 3 February 2026 that challenges around, inter alia, on-site nursing provision created a risk to the safe running of summer schemes in 2026.  The Department of Health advised my department that it would not be able to provide on-site nursing provision for special school summer schemes on 24 March 2026.  Following my direction, officials in the Education Authority liaised with the Department of Health and concerns regarding on-site nursing provision to support the safe running of Special School Summer Schemes for 2026 have now been addressed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the date on which the Department of Health advised that required nursing cover would not be available for special schools’ summer schemes for 2026.
answer_text: Officials advised me on 3 February 2026 that challenges around, inter alia, on-site nursing provision created a risk to the safe running of summer schemes in 2026.  The Department of Health advised my department that it would not be able to provide on-site nursing provision for special school summer schemes on 24 March 2026.  Following my direction, officials in the Education Authority liaised with the Department of Health and concerns regarding on-site nursing provision to support the safe running of Special School Summer Schemes for 2026 have now been addressed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health why his Department were unable to offer the Education Authority and the Department of Education any solutions to ensure the continuation of special schools' summer schemes.
answer_text: I refer the member to the response provided for AQW 43417/22-27.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail (i) the current status of the publication of the Historical Clerical Child Abuse report; and (ii) the date on which their Department will publish the full research and recommendations.
answer_text: We are considering the findings of the research into Historical Clerical Child Abuse. Officials remain committed to engaging with victims and survivors to ensure they are kept informed of developments.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission to detail (i) the current maximum seating capacity of the Blue Flax restaurant in Parliament Buildings; and (ii) how this compares with the capacity prior to its renovation.
answer_text: Both prior to and following refurbishment, the Blue Flax restaurant accommodates up to 130 people at any one time.  However, the new design provides more flexible space for working and meetings, enabling it to be used by greater numbers more frequently throughout the working day.The re-design of the Blue Flax also provides improved accessibility and a more inclusive experience for people with disabilities. The space is now more adaptable and fit for purpose to support a modern working environment.  There has been a noticeable increase in people using it for work purposes and informal meetings - a significant advantage when there is a high demand for meeting space within the building. This has been facilitated by the introduction of flexible seating arrangements accommodating a variety of configurations.  For example, under the previous arrangement, tables designed for six were frequently occupied by only two or three customers. In contrast, the current set-up allows tables to be split or combined as necessary, helping to optimise overall seating capacity.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether any fee is paid to contractors specifically for putting out and collecting signs on the road.
answer_text: For most small-scale works instructed by my Department, the cost of placing and removing the required signage is not individually priced but included within the overall scope of the tendered works.  For larger or more complex works, individual items are included in the contract to cover costs. Payment terms and associated costs are determined by the contracted rates agreed through a competitive tendering process.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any preparatory work their Department has undertaken on the budget, cost modelling and financial planning for a statutory public inquiry into clerical sexual abuse.
answer_text: We are considering the findings of the research into Historical Clerical Child Abuse.Officials remain committed to keeping victims and survivors informed throughout this process.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any assessment their Department has made of the policy and governance risks arising from the delay of over ten years in progressing the Interdepartmental Working Group’s findings on Historical Clerical Child Abuse.
answer_text: The Interdepartmental Working Group (IDWG) was set up in 2017 to consider and take forward work on historic Mother and Baby Homes and Magdalene Laundries and Historical Clerical Child Abuse (HCCA) which fell outside the terms of reference of the Historical Institutional Abuse Inquiry.  The IDWG’s work on Mother and Baby Homes and Magdalene Laundries concluded in 2021 and further work in that area has been taken forward under the Truth Recovery Programme since then.A Reference Group of HCCA Victims and Survivors was established, which serves as both a conduit for enabling the voice of victims and survivors to inform the work of the IDWG as well as keeping victims and survivors informed of the progress of the work.  The Reference Group played a key role in developing the Terms of Reference for the three research projects commissioned by the IDWG in 2024 and worked alongside the IDWG to review the research findings and develop high-level recommendations.  The research into Historical Clerical Child Abuse was completed in 2025.   We are currently considering the way forward.Officials remain committed to keeping victims and survivors informed of developments.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail whether local councils have the legal authority to extend Sunday trading hours beyond the limits set out in the Shops (Sunday Trading &c.) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997.
answer_text: I refer the member to the answer to AQW 26733/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any estimate that has been made of the cost of extending bowel cancer screening to those aged 50–59, including how this compares to the potential savings from earlier diagnosis.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail the reason why Northern Ireland remains the only region of the United Kingdom not to offer bowel cancer screening to those aged 50–59.

To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any assessment that has been made of the public consultations on extending Sunday trading hours to ensure they accurately reflect the legal constraints under existing legislation.
answer_text: No assessment has been made.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the legislative changes that would be required to extend Sunday trading hours across Belfast beyond the framework set out in the Shops (Sunday Trading &c.) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997.
answer_text: Any amendment to the Shops (Sunday Trading &c.) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 would require primary legislation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he has any plans to extend bowel cancer screening to those aged 50–59, including any associated timeframes.

To ask the Minister of Health to detail any assessment he has made of the impact on patient outcomes of not offering bowel cancer screening to those aged 50–59, compared to other regions of the United Kingdom.

To ask the Minister of Justice, following reports that football banning orders issued in Northern Ireland have been deemed unlawful due to legislative deficiencies, to detail (i) how this situation arose; (ii) the number of cases that are affected; and (ii) any steps being taken to rectify the issue and restore confidence in the operation of football banning orders.
answer_text: Having secured legal advice on this matter, I am satisfied that the continued operation of the Football Banning Order powers, and associated sports ground offence provisions contained in the 2011 Act, is lawful by virtue of the “always speaking” provision of the Interpretation Act (NI) 1954. This operates to prevent statutes from becoming outdated, including in instances such as this where organisations and sports championships are renamed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any action he is taking to address the inadequate number of spaces at Loughgall Playgroup.
answer_text: The Education Authority Pre-School Education Group (EA PEG) is responsible for managing the Pre-School Education Programme (PSEP) and for ensuring that sufficient funded pre-school education provision is available in all areas to provide a funded pre-school education place for every target age child whose parents want it.  DE plays no role in this process.The EA has advised that the provisional Pupil Allocation Number (PAN) given to Loughgall Playgroup at the outset of the admissions process has been increased to enable the setting to admit additional applicants.  All children in the area that applied for a funded pre-school education place have received an offer of a funded place in a setting of their parents’ preference and sufficient alternative provision also remains available in the area.The EA will continue to review levels of pre-school provision in all areas throughout the admissions process and may increase PANs for some settings where it is appropriate to do so.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the reasons why bowel cancer screening in Northern Ireland is only offered to those aged 60–74, while the rest of the United Kingdom offers screening from age 50.

To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail a timeline for the conclusion of his Department’s ongoing consideration of the Loughs Agency’s staffing position, including when he expects to provide a definitive answer to the Assembly.
answer_text: While there is ongoing engagement by my officials with the Agency, the Department of Climate, Energy and the Environment, and NSMC Joint Secretariat on issues relating to the Agency’s staffing position, based on the most recent information provided by the Loughs Agency, my officials consider that the Agency is operating in line with its approved complement. This position will continue to be monitored and considered throughout the year, alongside the need for a staff complement review to be brought forward to the NSMC for consideration.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail (i) the total cost of engaging a private photographer to accompany the deputy First Minister on her recent visit to Washington DC; and (ii) any competitive procurement process that was followed in making that appointment.
answer_text: The total cost of engaging the photographer was £4,714.40, which included travel and accommodation costs. These costs are part shared with the Department for Communities.    The appointment was made in line with NICS procurement policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to clarify how his statement that there are no plans to bring the Loughs Agency’s science and corporate services into his Department aligns with his further statement that this matter remains under ongoing consideration by officials.
answer_text: The wording used in this AQW question above relates to separate responses provided in response to two separate parts of a previous AQW asked.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to outline the amount of money spent within her Department on ending violence against women and girls in each of the last two years.

To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, given that he previously confirmed that the Loughs Agency must operate within an NSMC-approved staffing complement, and that any changes require formal approval, why, more than a year after this issue was first raised, his Department is still liaising with the Loughs Agency rather than directing it to comply with the approved complement.
answer_text: My officials have advised the Loughs Agency of the need to ensure it operates within the approved complement. They also continue to liaise with officials in the Department of Climate, Energy and the Environment, North South Ministerial Council (NSMC) and the Agency on the Agency’s staffing complement figure and the need for it to be reviewed.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) the specific matters that are currently under liaison between his Department, the Loughs Agency and other bodies in relation to staffing; and (ii) whether any breach of the approved staffing complement has been identified.
answer_text: My officials are liaising with the Loughs Agency, Department of Climate, Energy and the Environment and the North South Ministerial Council in relation to the interpretation and application of the staffing complement as approved in 2006; how staffing is reported at points in time and over time periods; and how absences from key posts can be covered by temporary staff.  Officials are also liaising with these bodies in relation to a review of the staff complement. (ii)	It has not been determined that the Agency has exceeded its complement.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether the Loughs Agency is currently operating within, at or above its approved staffing complement.
answer_text: Based on the most recent information provided by the Loughs Agency, my officials consider that the Agency is operating in line with its approved complement. This position will continue to be monitored and considered throughout the year.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) any enforcement action available to his Department where the Loughs Agency operates outside its approved staffing complement; and (ii) whether any such action has been considered or taken in the last 12 months.
answer_text: It has not been identified that my Department needs to consider, or take, any enforcement action in relation to this matter. None has been taken.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, given that the Loughs Agency has stated that 11 additional posts were created within its existing complement, to detail the steps his Department has taken to independently verify this.
answer_text: My officials have sought information from Loughs Agency in relation to the posts referenced.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 39823/22-27, when a consultancy was commissioned to undertake a Project Forestry Review of the TREES Project, whether (i) at any stage the consultancy expressed an interest in interviewing the manager of the TREES project; (ii) such an interview took place; and (iii) the Loughs Agency instructed, advised or intimated that the consultancy should not interview the manager of the TREES project.
answer_text: The Agency has advised that this report constitutes a technical evaluation conducted by a Chartered Forester. As the question concerns employment matters, it would not be appropriate to comment further.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) the guidance within his Department in terms of responding to press inquiries from media organisations; and (ii) whether the guidance advises officials to treat GB News, or any other news outlet, differently from others.
answer_text: The Department applies a standard and established process when responding to enquiries from the media, with the final decision on how the Department will proceed resting with the DAERA Minister on the basis of each individual request.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 42146/22-27, to detail (i) when his Department sought this assurance; and (ii) any assessment that has been made of the situation, now that assurance has been provided.
answer_text: The Department sought this assurance on 23 February 2026.The Department has reviewed the assurance provided by Foras na Gaeilge and is content that the organisation’s governance framework remains sufficient to ensure that all funding provided to Conradh na Gaeilge is applied only to agreed language-related activities. The Department will continue to monitor this through the established oversight and governance arrangements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to provide a detailed breakdown of the total cost of the remedial works at Glenshesk Road, Ballycastle, following the landslide in Easter 2024.
answer_text: As the works were substantially complete by the end of January 2026, a number of final payments remain to be submitted and reviewed. The overall cost is expected to be in the region of £950k to £1m.The requested breakdown in financial information is considered commercially sensitive. Disclosure of such data could enable others, particularly economic competitors, to identify commercial rates and costs associated with these works, which may potentially undermine the legitimate economic interests of the Department and its contractors.Once the scheme is complete, my Department will be able to provide the overall cost of the work.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission whether any tax arising from purchases made in Parliament Buildings is paid to the Irish Republic rather than the Exchequer of the United Kingdom.
answer_text: Aramark, the current support services contractor in Parliament Buildings (catering, cleaning, porterage and associated helpdesk services), has confirmed that all income generated through the Northern Ireland Assembly is traded through Aramark in Northern Ireland, and that all resulting tax is paid to the Exchequer of the United Kingdom.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to specify any practical actions their Department proposes to take when it identifies what it considers to be misinformation or a harmful narrative, including whether this will involve monitoring, public messaging, engagement with media organisations, or any form of intervention in public debate.

To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the criteria, guidance or evidential standards that will be used by their Department when identifying or classifying information as misinformation, disinformation or a harmful narrative.
answer_text: A range of guidance and frameworks are used across government as part of wider consideration around accuracy, intent and context. This forms part of the accepted definitions as follows:  Misinformation is false, inaccurate, or misleading information that is shared without the intent to deceive.Disinformation is false information that is created and shared deliberately to mislead, manipulate, or cause harm.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail who in their Department will determine what constitutes misinformation or a harmful narrative for the purposes of the draft Framework for Race Relations.
answer_text: The draft Framework for Race Relations is intended to guide work in the years ahead, for a shared vision of a society where diversity is valued and where everyone can live free from discrimination.As part of our ongoing work to enhance cohesion within local communities, we will strive to make sure discussions are informed and evidence based.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 41777/22-27, to detail whether there is a difference applied to how requests are treated during the process depending on the media organisation involved.
answer_text: All media enquiries are considered on a case-by-case basis, taking into account the nature of the request.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of clinicians currently working within gender identity services in Northern Ireland, broken down by (i) specialty; and (ii) Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The Regional Gender Identity Knowing Our Identity (KOI) service currently has 0.2 WTE Consultant Psychiatrist, 1 WTE Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (CAMHS) Clinical Nurse Specialist and 0.2 WTE Consultant Paediatric Endocrinologist. The Adult service currently has 0.2 WTE Locum Consultant Psychiatrist and 1.4 WTE Band 7 Gender Therapists.The Regional Gender Identity service is provided in Belfast Trust.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail who currently holds overall clinical responsibility for gender identity services in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Belfast Health and Social Care Trust hold overall clinical responsibility for Gender Identity Services in Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission why a member of an MLA's staff had their Apple Pay blocked due to a transaction in the Blue Flax restaurant being flagged by his bank as being outside the UK.
answer_text: In September 2025 some connectivity and payment issues were reported in relation to the coffee machine in the Blue Flax, which had been repositioned and fitted with a self-service payment module that had been incorrectly configured. This was recognised as a possible source of issue with Apple Pay and the configuration was promptly corrected that week.Support Services have recently been made aware that some banks were flagging some transactions made in sterling across multiple outlets in Parliament Buildings as being foreign transactions. Support Services immediately engaged with the Support Services contractor to seek an urgent resolution to this issue. The Support Services contractor identified that a configuration issue had occurred on their bank’s merchant services side and this was resolved on 20 March 2026. Support Services are awaiting a full report from the Support Services contractor outlining what occurred, why any changes were made and what controls will be implemented to ensure this will not happen again.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission for a full breakdown of the amount it has provided for the Independent Assembly Members' Renumeration Board to date.
answer_text: The Assembly Commission has provided £6,481.44 in member fees, £721.44 in travel costs and £202.27 in refreshments for the Independent Remuneration Board up to and including 13 March 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to outline any safeguards that exist to ensure that children presenting with gender-related distress receive a full assessment for co-existing mental health, neurodevelopmental or psychosocial conditions prior to referral to gender identity services.

To ask the Assembly Commission to detail the number of occasions that people have reported being charged a transaction fee for being abroad after they used the Blue Flax restaurant in Parliament Buildings.
answer_text: No customers have reported being charged a foreign transaction fee following the use of the Blue Flax restaurant.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to place (i) all departmental and Invest NI notes, briefings and correspondence relating to the New York meeting with Cantor Fitzgerald; and (ii) all documents on the subsequent decision by the company to pull out of the Belfast investment, in the Assembly Library.
answer_text: Depositing papers in the Assembly Library serves the function of placing information in the public domain. The documents include commercially sensitive information and if released would likely prejudice our economic interests. Therefore, it would not be appropriate to do so.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) any assessment that has been made of the collapse of the Cantor Fitzgerald 300-job investment; and (ii) whether she has conducted any review into the handling of her meeting with the company and its aftermath.
answer_text: Not all prospective investors progress to an announced project. Investments can change or be withdrawn at any stage, and proposed project plans do not proceed for a variety of reasons. Announcements are made only after agreements are finalised. All prospective investor meetings are held on a confidential basis to protect commercially sensitive information and respect the privacy of all parties.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the measures he has taken to ensure marriage is promoted inside the Northern Ireland curriculum since coming into office.
answer_text: Schools may shape RSE in line with their ethos and may include teaching about the values of marriage, whilst remaining within statutory curriculum requirements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail why a project described by Invest NI as a done deal, involving 300 jobs in Belfast and a signed letter of offer for public funding, collapsed shortly after her critical meeting with Cantor Fitzgerald in New York.
answer_text: I have no record of the project being described by Invest NI as a “done deal”. It is for companies to explain their investment decisions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on resolving industrial action with the Northern Ireland Ambulance Service to ensure category 2 ambulance calls are responded to in the last hour of a shift.
To ask the Assembly Commission to detail the measures that (i) have been taken; and (ii) are planned, to clean the steps at the front of Parliament Buildings.
answer_text: A specialist clean of the front steps was carried out in August 2022. Since then, low-pressure washing and the removal of weeds have been undertaken as required to maintain the condition of the stonework.Further cleaning, using specialist conservation techniques, is scheduled for April this year. Where necessary, targeted treatment will be applied to remove staining and ingrained dirt, ensuring the continued preservation and appearance of the stone.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any estimate his Department has made of the number of classroom assistant posts that may be affected, reduced or removed as a direct consequence of the proposed changes to special educational needs provision, including the move away from one-to-one support.
answer_text: I refer the Member to the answer to AQW 42353/22-27 published in the Weekly Answer Booklet for week ending 20 March 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether his Department has carried out any impact assessment of the proposed changes to special educational needs provision on (i) classroom assistant redundancies; (ii) reductions in contracted hours; and (iii) the redeployment of classroom assistants.
answer_text: The Education Authority has advised that the Enhanced Support Model:(i) will not require classroom assistant (CA) redundancies;(ii) will not require reductions in contracted hours; and(iii) may enable greater flexibility to redeploy CA to most effectively meet the needs of children and young people with special educational needs.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) any assessment his Department has made of the implications of the proposed changes to special educational needs provision for (a) classroom assistant workload; and (b) safeguarding responsibilities within the classroom; and (ii) whether any changes will be made to the terms, conditions or pay scales of classroom assistants as a result.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised that:(i) A public consultation on proposed changes to Special Educational Needs provision will open on 24 March 2026. This will be used to shape a new model of support, which seeks to ensure that schools can most effectively allocate resources to best meet the needs of children and young people with SEN in their school.This will allow for more effective use of classroom assistants, and that changes can be made to ensure appropriate safeguarding measures can be put in place.(ii) The Enhanced Support Model will develop the classroom assistant role, enabling greater opportunity for specialisation, and career progression pathways.  Work will also be taken forward to design a future model for Classroom Assistant employment and career pathways, drawing on best practice, lived experiences and emerging educational needs.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the estimated additional cost in 2026-27 of implementing a 3.3 per cent pay award for Agenda for Change staff.
answer_text: The estimated cost of implementing the recommended 3.3 percent pay award for all AfC staff groups in Northern Ireland would be £151million.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how data collected by her Department and its arm’s-length bodies is broken down by gender, detailing (i) each category used; (ii) the definitions applied to each category; and (iii) whether each category is recorded on the basis of biological sex or self-identified gender.
answer_text: Data on sex or gender are collected in different ways across my Department and its arm’s-length bodies.For statistical data, DfI survey data collected by the NI Statistics and Research Agency (NISRA) are aligned with current guidance from the Office for Statistics regulation. Survey owners are encouraged to collect sex and/or gender from respondents based on their specific needs and to be clear about what data have been collected when publishing survey data.The Government Statistical Service harmonisation team, based at the Office for National Statistics, are undertaking work to develop best practice harmonised standards for sex and gender identity data. DfI statisticians, alongside NISRA, will consider this guidance – and any changes required for data collection – when it is published.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice how data collected by her Department and its arm’s-length bodies is broken down by gender, detailing (i) each category used; (ii) the definitions applied to each category; and (iii) whether each category is recorded on the basis of biological sex or self-identified gender.
answer_text: While the Department utilises a wide range of statistics from across the criminal justice system as part of its broader evidence base in delivering its objectives, it is the responsibility of each criminal justice organisation, including Departmental Arm’s Length Bodies, to ensure the quality of the information produced by them is fit for purpose.  You may, therefore, wish to direct any further query relating to practices used in the recording of crime data to the individual organisation concerned.  In relation to data collected by the Department itself, information is collected to inform data systems in Youth Justice Agency (YJA), Northern Ireland Prison Service (NIPS), Forensic Science Northern Ireland (FSNI), Compensation Agency and Northern Ireland Courts and Tribunals Service (NICTS). The NICTS main data collection system has the option to record the following: Male /Female/ Unknown/ Blank.For criminal cases, the information is uploaded from Causeway and shared with the NICTS operating system.For Civil and Family cases, the data is collected by NICTS from the information recorded on the application form submitted to the court office by either the applicant or the respondent in the case.  There is no standard question asked at court offices and if gender is not specifically referenced in the application documents it is not recorded.Where responses are reported to NICTS, they refer to the persons gender.In the NIPS, the Prisoner Records Information System (PRISM) identifies a prisoner’s gender as: Male, Female or Transgender. In relation to the YJA, information is collected through its Equality Monitoring Form. Respondents are asked to select one of the following options:•	Male•	Female•	Other Responses are recorded according to the individual’s self-identified gender.Compensation Agency applications record 3 options against Gender: -•	Male•	Female•	OtherThis selection is mandatory in online applications.  However, paper-based applications can leave this selection blank.Where the Department utilises survey data collated by NISRA, such as the Safe Community Survey, it is reassured that NISRA survey data collections are aligned with current guidance from the Office for Statistics regulation. Survey owners are encouraged to collect sex and/or gender from respondents based on their specific needs and to be clear about what data have been collected when publishing survey data. Currently, information collated and published as part of the Safe Community Survey, which is published by the Department, is based on the following categories and definitions:(i) Categories used - Male/female breakdown. Respondents can refuse to answer.(ii) Definitions applied to each category -Based on sex at birth.(iii) Whether each category is recorded on the basis of biological sex or self-identified gender - A further, separate gender identity question is included later in the survey that asks the respondent ‘Is the gender you identify with the same as the sex you were re

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 39823/22-27, when a consultancy was commissioned to undertake a Governance and Oversight Review of the TREES Project, whether (i) at any stage did the consultancy express an interest in interviewing the manager of the TREES project; (ii) such an interview took place; (iii) the Loughs Agency instructed, advised or intimate that the consultancy should not interview the manager of the TREES project.
answer_text: The Agency has advised that this relates to employment matters which would not be appropriate to comment on.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any guidance that exists governing communications between the Minister, or her office, and operational officers of the Police Service of Northern Ireland concerning matters relating to political parties or political communications.
answer_text: The Police Service of Northern Ireland (PSNI) is operationally independent of my Department and is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board.  Communications on matters relating to political parties or political communications are addressed by the PSNI, as necessary, and there is no requirement for any formal guidance.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the number of times she has contacted officers of the Police Service of Northern Ireland in relation to the content of political party statements, press releases or public commentary since taking office.
answer_text: Where the content of political party statements, press releases or public commentary touch on matters relevant to my responsibilities as Minister of Justice, I may occasionally alert the Police Service of Northern Ireland (PSNI) where I consider it necessary to do so.  I have never sought to influence or interfere with the operational independence of the PSNI in how they respond to such matters.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance how data collected by his Department and its arm’s-length bodies is broken down by gender, detailing (i) each category used; (ii) the definitions applied to each category; and (iii) whether each category is recorded on the basis of biological sex or self-identified gender.
answer_text: As Finance Minister, my department has responsibility for the Civil Service workforce which includes collection and reporting of workforce equality data to the Equality Commission to meet statutory reporting obligations under the Fair Employment and Treatment (NI) Order 1998 (‘the 1998 Order’) and as part of equality best practice to identify areas of under-representation.  The obligations require collection and reporting of data on the number of employees and job applicants who are (i) male; (ii) female - there is no express definition within the 1998 Order and the Sex Discrimination Order 1976.	For statistical data, NISRA survey data collections are aligned with current guidance from the Office for Statistics regulation. Survey owners are encouraged to collect sex and/or gender from respondents based on their specific needs and to be clear about what data have been collected when publishing survey data. The Government Statistical Service (GSS) harmonisation team, based at the Office for National Statistics, are undertaking work to develop best practice harmonised standards for sex and gender identity data. NISRA will consider this guidance when it is published.In relation to the Special EU Programme Body (SEUPB) which is a North/South Body with responsibility for implementation of the PEACE PLUS Programme, the categories used for programme related data are: Male Female Non-binary I prefer to self-describe as ___________________________ I prefer not to say For SEUPB staff related data the categories used are (i) male; (ii) female. For both sets of data, SEUPB does not apply any definitions to the categories, and each category is recorded on the basis of self-identified gender.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how data collected by his Department and its arm’s-length bodies is broken down by gender, detailing (i) each category used; (ii) the definitions applied to each category; and (iii) whether each category is recorded on the basis of biological sex or self-identified gender.
answer_text: The HSC Data Dictionary has definitions for both gender and sex (also known as Sex at Birth, Natal Sex or Person Phenotypic Sex). Codes / Descriptors used within the systems are M-Male, F-Female, X-X(indeterminate), U-Unknown.Systems currently record this information in different ways, with some capturing biological sex and others capturing selfidentified gender. Work is ongoing to review these differences and to inform a recommended standardised approach, for example recording biological sex alongside an optional field for self-identified gender. Any change to current practice would be likely to require system updates, as well as alignment and agreement across healthcare professionals.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) whether he has undertaken any review of his Department’s funding relationship with Conradh na Gaeilge, in light of the fact that the organisation has amended its constitution in relation to an all Ireland state; and (ii) whether this political stance impacts current or future funding.
answer_text: The Department does not have a direct funding relationship with Conradh na Gaeilge. DfC allocates resources to Foras na Gaeilge, who in turn provide funding to Conradh, one of the 6 Lead Organisations that deliver agreed programmes and activities on its behalf.		The funding of Conradh is an operational matter for Foras and its Board. However, Foras have provided the Department with assurance that a robust governance framework underpins its allocation to Conradh and have confirmed that all monies provided to Conradh are used solely for language-related activities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how data collected by his Department and its arm’s-length bodies is broken down by gender, detailing (i) each category used; (ii) the definitions applied to each category; and (iii) whether each category is recorded on the basis of biological sex or self-identified gender.
answer_text: Data on sex and gender identity are collected in different ways across the department and its arm’s length bodies, reflecting differing business and analytical requirements. In relation to pupils, the department collects information via the school’s management system, on the basis of sex, using the categories male and female. Data on gender identity is not collected from pupils by the department.  Similarly, data relating to teachers is extracted from the Teachers' Payroll System which includes the variable 'sex of teacher'. There are two categories used, male and female, and these are defined on the basis of biological sex.Some arm’s-length bodies have advised that when collecting staff related data they include additional gender categories beyond male and female, such as non-binary or other. The Government Statistical Service (GSS) harmonisation team, based at the Office for National Statistics, are undertaking work to develop best practice harmonised standards for the collection and reporting of sex and gender identity data. Departmental statisticians, working with NISRA colleagues, will consider these standards, and any implications for future advice to colleagues, once they are published.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the amount of public funding provided to Conradh na Gaeilge (i) directly by the Department for Communities; and (ii) indirectly through Foras na Gaeilge or any other arm’s-length body funded by the Department, in each of the last ten financial years, including (a) the purpose of each funding stream; and (b) any funding currently being received by the organisation.
answer_text: The table below sets out the amount of public funding provided to Conradh na Gaeilge over the last ten financial years, comprising (i) any direct funding from the Department for Communities; (ii) any indirect funding provided through Foras na Gaeilge or other Departmental arm’slength bodies; along with (a) the purpose of each funding stream; and (b) details of any current funding:Financial YearFunding SourceAmount (£)PurposeCurrent year (2025/26)DfCNone-Foras na Gaeilge£871,564Lead Organisation funding / Scholarships SchemeArts Council NI£4,889New Small Grants Programme - Ár Logainmneacha Comhroinnte2024/25DfC£3,154  JobStart scheme Foras na Gaeilge£760,539Lead Organisation funding / Scholarships Scheme2023/24DfCNone-Foras na Gaeilge£773,140Lead Organisation funding / Scholarships Scheme2022/23DfC £5,293 Languages Capital Fund Pilot Programme Foras na Gaeilge£794,185Lead Organisation funding / Scholarships Scheme2021/22DfC£10,600 Languages Capital Fund Pilot Programme Foras na Gaeilge£771,508Lead Organisation funding / Scholarships Scheme2020/21DfC£647,500  Various Covid support related programmesForas na Gaeilge£707,664Lead Organisation funding / Scholarships Scheme2019/20DfCNone-Foras na Gaeilge£747,939Lead Organisation funding / Scholarships Scheme2018/19DfCNone-Foras na Gaeilge£732,568Lead Organisation funding / Scholarships Scheme2017/18DfC£1,070Líofa Intensive CoursesForas na Gaeilge£728,748Lead Organisation funding / Scholarships Scheme2016/17DfCNone-Foras na Gaeilge£726,716Lead Organisation funding / Scholarships Scheme2015/16DfCNone-Foras na Gaeilge£735,727Lead Organisation funding / Scholarships Scheme

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how data collected by her Department and its arm’s-length bodies is broken down by gender, detailing (i) each category used; (ii) the definitions applied to each category; and (iii) whether each category is recorded on the basis of biological sex or self-identified gender.
answer_text: The majority of the data collected on sex and gender identity relates to students at Further Education Colleges and Higher Education Institutions.At the request of the Department for the Economy (DfE), Further Education colleges ask enrolling students questions on sex and gender identity. The sex question mirrors the 2021 Census (“What is your sex?”) with ‘Female’ and ‘Male’ response options. The gender identity question follows the current harmonised wording (“Is the gender you identify with the same as your sex registered at birth?”) with ‘Yes’, ‘No’ and ‘Prefer not to say’ options.Similarly, at the request of statutory customers including DfE, the Higher Education Statistics Authority (HESA) collects sex and gender identity data for students and staff across UK higher education providers. The sex question mirrors the 2021 Census and includes ‘Female’, ‘Male’ and ‘Other’. HESA guidance states that ‘Other’ should be used only for a third sex legally recognised by another country. The gender identity question uses the same harmonised wording and response options, with guidance advising respondents to use the sex recorded on legal documents such as a birth certificate, Gender Recognition Certificate or passport.The Government Statistical Service (GSS) Sex and Gender Identity Harmonisation team is currently undertaking work to develop best practice harmonised standards for the collection of sex and gender identity data. Part of this work includes exploring user needs for data on these topics, to ensure that new standards are fit for purpose and meet the needs of data users. Officials in my department are engaging with the GSS on this work.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs how data collected by his Department and its arm’s-length bodies is broken down by gender, detailing (i) each category used; (ii) the definitions applied to each category; and (iii) whether each category is recorded on the basis of biological sex or self-identified gender.
answer_text: Staff in my department align any data collection with guidance from the ‘Office for Statistics Regulation’. Survey owners collect sex and/or gender from respondents based on their specific needs and are clear about what data has been collected when publishing it. The Government Statistical Service harmonisation team, based at the Office for National Statistics, are undertaking work to develop best practice harmonised standards for sex and gender identity data. DAERA will review this guidance, and any changes required to NISRA’s approach, when it is published.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister how data collected by their Department and its arm’s-length bodies is broken down by gender, detailing (i) each category used; (ii) the definitions applied to each category; and (iii) whether each category is recorded on the basis of biological sex or self-identified gender.

To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 37464/22-27, to detail whether members of the Gender Identity Review Group and the Gender Identity Liaison Panel were required to complete declarations of interests, including any declared affiliations with advocacy organisations, campaigning groups or charities relating to transgender policy or gender identity services.
answer_text: Members of the Gender Identity Review Group and the Gender Identity Liaison Panel were not required to complete declarations of interests as their remit was to review a pathway which did not make any commercial/procurement decisions.It should be noted however that a declaration of interest was sought from members at the outset of each meeting.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how data collected by this Department and its arm’s-length bodies is broken down by gender, detailing (i) each category used; (ii) the definitions applied to each category; and (iii) whether each category is recorded on the basis of biological sex or self-identified gender.
answer_text: My Department’s approach to the collection and storage of data is compliant with guidance issued by the Office for Statistics Regulation.  Data on sex and/or gender are collected only where there is a clear and specific business or analytical need.  The Government Statistical Service harmonisation team, based at the Office for National Statistics, is undertaking work to develop best practice harmonised standards for the collection and reporting of sex and gender identity data.  My Departmental statisticians, working with NISRA colleagues, will consider these standards and any implications once they are published.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission whether any record exists of the total staff time spent by Assembly Commission staff in supporting the Committee on Standards and Privileges in its consideration of complaint NIA103/22-27 against Mr Timothy Gaston MLA, including any estimates, internal records and case management data relating to the number of staff hours devoted to the matter.
answer_text: Assembly committees routinely deal with multiple matters in parallel. Staff who support committees therefore work across a range of matters on an ongoing basis, often on the same day. Staff are neither required nor expected to record the time spent on individual items of committee business.    As a consequence, the Assembly Commission does not hold any record of the staff time spent in supporting the Committee on Standards and Privileges in its consideration of complaint NIA103/22-27.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether their Department plans to commission a prevalence study to determine the (i) scale; and (ii) long-term impact of child abuse in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: We met with the Commissioner for Survivors of Institutional Childhood Abuse on 23 February. During the meeting we discussed a range of issues, including her proposal for research into the prevalence of non-recent child sexual abuse.Plans for such research will be kept under review as part of our consideration of the overall research needs of the department.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any provision that currently exists within government to advocate for survivors of childhood abuse who fall outside the statutory remit of the Commissioner for Survivors of Institutional Childhood Abuse.
answer_text: Supporting victims and survivors, including survivors of childhood abuse is a central priority for The Executive Office. Our commitment is delivered through a number of work areas including the Strategy for Victims and Survivors, the Strategy to End Violence Against Women and Girls, the Truth Recovery Programme and the dedicated initiatives taken forward through the Communities in Transition programme.The Department of Justice also delivers a broad range of support for all victims of crime, including specialist advocacy through ASSIST NI, independent legal advice via the Sexual Offences Legal Adviser services, and emotional and practical assistance provided by Victim Support NI.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail (i) whether they plan to retain the Office of the Commissioner for Survivors of Institutional Childhood Abuse beyond December 2027; and (ii) any work currently underway within their Department to determine the future remit of the office.

answer_text: The Department was made aware by an MLA in November 2024 of some concerns raised with her in relation to operational matters at AFBI. These concerns did not include animal welfare or environmental matters and were passed to the AFBI Chief Executive to deal with.In June 2025 the Chief Executive advised the Department that AFBI had received concerns in relation to animal welfare issues at Hillsborough and that these were being examined. A DAERA veterinary/animal health inspection was carried out in June 2025. A further veterinary/animal health inspection and a NIEA inspection were carried out when press queries were subsequently raised in October 2025.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) any inspection and regulatory regime that applies to Agri-Food and Biosciences Institute (AFBI) facilities; (ii) how this compares with the regime applied to private farms; and (iii) the number of inspections that have been carried out at AFBI Hillsborough in each of the last five years, including whether any enforcement action has been taken.
answer_text: AFBI are subject to the same risk-based inspection regime as other farm businesses. In instances where non-compliance is reported, the appropriate authority will investigate and initiate action where necessary which may include unannounced inspections.The same legislative obligations and inspection regime apply to all private farms.Over the past five years, the AFBI Hillsborough site has been subject to a range of inspections by various teams within the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (DAERA). During 2025, the Veterinary Service Animal Health Group undertook two inspections at the AFBI Hillsborough site. These inspections were initiated following receipt of welfare complaints. Upon investigation, no welfare concerns were identified at the site, and as such, no enforcement action was deemed necessary.Within the past five years, the Agri-food Inspection Branch of the Veterinary Service Animal Health Group conducted a total of five separate inspections at Hillsborough. In one instance, informal enforcement action was taken in response to minor regulatory requirements, with compliance subsequently achieved. The Northern Ireland Environment Agency (NIEA) regularly inspects the Anaerobic Digestion plant at the Hillsborough site, carrying out two visits per year. Across these inspections, no enforcement action has been required. In addition to routine inspections, the NIEA has investigated four reports of water pollution in the vicinity of the Hillsborough site, 2 discovered by NIEA and 2 self-reported by AFBI. These reports were investigated and classified as having a Low Severity environmental impact which did not reach NIEA’s threshold for formal enforcement action to be taken. As with any other business sector, where a Low Severity incident is confirmed, NIEA ensures that the pollution has stopped, seeks action to be taken and/or provides advice to minimise the risk of recurrence.It is crucial that any reports of suspected non-compliance are submitted promptly through the appropriate reporting channels, as this enables inspectors to visit the site quickly and gather or validate the necessary evidence for potential enforcement
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any steps his Department is taking to enable men in Northern Ireland with high-risk non-metastatic prostate cancer to access abiraterone, given that the drug is now available for such patients in England, Scotland and Wales.
answer_text: My Department has a formal link with the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) under which NICE Technology Appraisals are reviewed locally for their legal and policy applicability here, and where found to be applicable, they are endorsed for implementation within Health and Social Care (HSC) organisations. This link has ensured that Northern Ireland has access to up-to-date, independent, professional, evidence-based guidance on the value of health care interventions. In practice this means that treatments that have been recommended by NICE for routine use in the NHS in England are also routinely available in Northern Ireland.Where NICE has recommended the use of abiraterone (Zytiga®) in the treatment of prostate cancer, this medicine is available to eligible patients in Northern Ireland, in line with the current NICE guidelines. Further information on this treatment is available at the following links:https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ta259https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ta387https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ta951https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ta1110My Department is aware that NHS England (NHSE) have recently adopted a commissioning position on the use of abiraterone (Zytiga®) for the treatment of high-risk, hormone-sensitive, non-metastatic prostate cancer in England based on the availability of a generic version of the medicine. Northern Ireland does not have its own health technology assessment body or equivalent to NICE, Scottish Medicines Consortium or All Wales Medicine Strategy Group, that could make a bespoke determination for Northern Ireland patients in the absence of NICE guidance. My Department has recently published a review of HSC medicines access arrangements which recognises the need to take action to ensure continued access to clinically and cost-effective medicines for the Northern Ireland population on an equitable basis, including in cases where NICE have not made a determination, but use is supported by clinical evidence and best practice. Informed by the review’s recommendations, my Department plans to review the existing workforce infrastructure underpinning medicines access pathways to identify potential resource requirements needed to strengthen existing access arrangements, including for use of off-label and off-patent medicines where use is supported by clinical evidence and best practice. Any additional resource requirements for medicines access infrastructure, and additional costs associated with provision of access for off-label or off-patent medicines, will need to be considered within the context of my Department’s challenging financial position and competing priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail (i) the total public sector headcount in Northern Ireland; and (ii) the equivalent figure per 1,000 population, compared to England
answer_text: The latest figures from the Office for National Statistics (ONS) indicate that there were 229,000 public sector employees in the North in September 2025.Based on the most recent mid-year population estimates for 2024, this equates to 119 public sector employees per 1,000 of the population in the North, compared to 85 in England.However, there are a number of issues which mean that such comparisons need to be treated with caution. The North’s level of relative need is higher, while some services in the public sector here are delivered by the private sector in England, for example, water utilities and some transport services. In addition, our size and rurality mean that we do not benefit from the same economies of scale in some areas of delivery.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether any Department has formally advised that its proposed 2026/27 allocation is insufficient to meet statutory or contractual obligations.
answer_text: The proposals I have brought forward for consultation have been developed against the backdrop of an exceptionally constrained financial position.I have been clear that they do not provide the level of funding I would wish to see directed at our public services; instead, they reflect what is possible within the funding available. In this context it has not been possible to provide any department with the funding it has requested. Each Minister must decide how they deliver their statutory or contractual obligations.I have met with each departmental Minister and am committed to working with my Executive colleagues to agree a multi-year Budget.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any modelling undertaken by his Department on the interaction between pay parity commitments and Barnett consequentials over the Budget period.
answer_text: Barnett consequentials are not hypothecated and it is for the Executive to determine how to allocate this funding in light of all competing priorities.  Individual Minsters are responsible for agreeing pay awards for staff groups within their remit with the key overarching principle of public sector pay policy being that pay must be affordable within each department’s budget settlement. It is essential that our vital public service workers are appropriately rewarded. Given the uncertainty of in year funding pay awards should not rely on significant sums being provided in year.  Instead, each Minister should ensure that appropriate pay awards are factored into budget plans from the start of the financial year, including the anticipated recommendations from pay review bodies.
