answer_text: 20 January 2025AQW 20678/22-27ANSWERA copy of the Northern Ireland Prison Service, Guidance on the care and management of transgender prisoners has been deposited in the Assembly Library.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of times (i) he; and (ii) his Special Adviser has been involved in clearing a response to a Freedom of Information request since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024.
answer_text: My Department’s Freedom of Information Procedures Manual sets out the roles and responsibilities of my officials in responding to requests. Neither my Special Adviser nor I, as Minister of Health, have any role in preparing or authorising responses to such requests.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of times (i) he; and (ii) his Special Adviser has been involved in clearing a response to a Freedom of Information request since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024.
answer_text: Since the restoration of the Executive in 2024, I have been involved in clearing one Freedom of Information request which sought a breakdown of Ministerial costs from 1st April 2024 to 31st July 2024. My Special Adviser has not been involved in clearing any Freedom of Information requests.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of times (i) he; and (ii) his Special Adviser has been involved in clearing a response to a Freedom of Information request since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024.
answer_text: My Special Advisor and I have not been involved in the clearance of Freedom of Information requests to the Department.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the number of times (i) they; and (ii) their Special Adviser has been involved in clearing a response to a Freedom of Information request since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024.
answer_text: We can confirm that there have been no occasions since the restoration of the Executive when we or our Special Advisors have been involved in clearing a response to a Freedom of Information request.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of times (i) he; and (ii) his Special Adviser has been involved in clearing a response to a Freedom of Information request since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024.
answer_text: I am not involved in clearing Departmental FOI responses, however on two occasions I have given my opinion as a qualified person under Section 36 of the Freedom of Information Act 2000.As the Minister for Communities I am Keeper of the Records for the Public Record Office of Northern Ireland (PRONI) and in this capacity have been involved in 43 Historical Freedom of Information requests.None

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of times (i) he; and (ii) his Special Adviser has been involved in clearing a response to a Freedom of Information request since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024.
answer_text: To date my Special Adviser and I have been involved in clearing one response to a Freedom of Information request since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of times (i) he; and (ii) his Special Adviser has been involved in clearing a response to a Freedom of Information request since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024.
answer_text: Since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024, neither I, nor my Special Adviser have been involved in clearing FOI responses within my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, since coming into office, whether she has contacted any police officer in regard to any allegation against Person A, which is not subject to any ongoing legal proceedings.
answer_text: As Justice Minister I am committed to respecting the operational independence of the PSNI, as I have made clear repeatedly. It would not, therefore, be appropriate for me to seek to become involved in or to influence any police investigation, other than in circumstances where I am either the complainant or a witness in the case.  I have not sought to do so in this or any other case.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission whether it has received the report from the Clerk/Chief Executive into the review of the use of allowances under the Assembly Members’ (Salaries and Expenses) Determination.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the outcome of the review of the Education Authority guidance into pupils being referred to by their preferred pronouns within the school setting, as announced by his predecessor in October 2022.
answer_text: My department does not currently have a policy in respect of the use of preferred pronouns and current operational guidance is a matter for the Education Authority. In light of the recent UK Supreme Court judgment regarding biological sex, I have asked my officials to consider the Department's policy in this area.  This may necessitate changes to the Education Authority's guidance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 15584/22-27, to detail any progress there has been on this matter.
answer_text: I have agreed to meet with those who have written to me about the establishment of a Harry Ferguson Museum. The Northern Ireland Museums Council will be represented at these meetings.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any action he is taking to prevent groups, known for performing pro-IRA songs, from performing in local libraries.
answer_text: Whilst the organisation of events and the engagement of performers is an operational matter for Libraries NI, it would of course be totally inappropriate for any sectarianism or the glorification of illegal terrorist organisations in these shared spaces. I recognise the important role libraries play in communities across Northern Ireland and that libraries are a safe place for both parents and children.  Libraries NI will not permit the hire of rooms for meetings and/or events which might compromise the perception of the library service as welcoming and inclusive.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 18943/22-27, AQW 19539/22-27 and AQW 19181/22-27, and given that his predecessor ordered a review of the Education Authority guidance into pupils being referred to by their preferred pronouns within the school setting, whether he will give this matter greater priority within the work of his Department.
answer_text: The HOPE Service is managed by the Education Authority (EA).  Any future review of the HOPE service or guidance would be a matter for the EA.

answer_text: This information can be derived from the Assembly Questions database available on the Northern Ireland Assembly website.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, in light of the recent court judgment in December 2024, to outline what action she is taking to ensure men are not imprisoned in the women's prison at Hydebank.

To ask the Assembly Commission, pursuant to AQW 18945/22-27, whether it will consider placing the collection of historic photographs from 1945 on display in Parliament Buildings to mark the 80th anniversary of the end of the Second World War.
answer_text: In line with his representational responsibilities, it will be for the Speaker to determine arrangements for the Assembly to mark the 80th anniversary of the end of the Second World War at Parliament Buildings, as was the case for the 80th anniversary of D-Day last year.  I understand that the 80th anniversary of VE Day is in the Speaker’s provisional programme of events and the Assembly Commission will consider any approaches made to it by the Speaker if it is asked to assist with the event plans.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the Barnet consequentials of the English national concessionary travel scheme.
answer_text: The Executive has not received any Barnett consequentials that were attributed to the English national concessionary travel scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the number of incidents of drone interference with prisons activity in the last three years.
answer_text: There have been six reported sightings of drones in the vicinity of a Northern Ireland prison in the last three years.  There was no impact upon the normal operation or the safety of prisons as a result of these incidents.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail (i) whether drones are banned from the vicinity of prisons; and (ii) any measures that will be put in place to prevent security breaches and privacy breaches at prisons.
answer_text: Drones are not banned from the vicinity of prisons in Northern Ireland, however the UK-wide Air Traffic Management and Unmanned Aircraft Act 2021 provides the Police Service of Northern Ireland and the Northern Ireland Prison Service with the authority to interfere with unmanned aircraft (drones) if they believe that those craft are engaged in the commission of an offence relating to prison security.I was fully supportive of UK Government plans earlier this year to address prison privacy issues through its Prison Media Bill.  This UK-wide Bill would have addressed drone use over prisons, particularly with regard to the recording and uploading of images.  I was disappointed that it did not complete its Parliamentary passage in advance of the 2024 General Election.  I understand that the Ministry of Justice hopes to re-introduce these measures if a suitable legislative vehicle can be identified.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she has made contact with an investigating officer in a live police investigation at any time since returning to office in February 2024.
answer_text: Investigation of crime is ultimately an operational matter for the Chief Constable, who is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board.  As I am committed to respecting the operational independence of both organisations, it would not be appropriate for me to become involved in live police investigations, other than in such circumstances where I am a complainant or witness.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether her Department benchmarks practice against best practice criteria outlined by Stonewall.
answer_text: The Civil Service participates in Stonewall’s Workplace Equality Index, which is a benchmarking tool to measure LGBTQ+ inclusion within an organisation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the membership of her Department’s LGBTQ+ Inclusion Steering Group.
answer_text: The Civil Service does not have an LGBTQ+ Inclusion Steering Group, nor does my department.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail where, in law, the official status of the English language in Northern Ireland is spelt out.
answer_text: The UK does not have a written constitution therefore there can be no express constitutional recognition of English as the official language. However, English is widely accepted as the official language of the UK.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the amount her Department has spent on Stonewall-related programmes in each of the last three years.
answer_text: The total spend incurred by my Department on Stonewall-related programmes in the last three years is outlined below:	2022/2023	2023/2024	Apr 2024 - date 	 Total	£2,950	£2,575	£2,875	£8,400

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether her Department is involved with the Stonewall Allies programme.
answer_text: My Department is not involved with the Stonewall Allies Programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he will embed accredited training to diagnose and treat arthritis into Northern Ireland's GP speciality training programme ST3 to improve referrals and bring down waiting lists.
answer_text: The GP Training programme at NIMDTA delivers the GP Training curriculum as stipulated by the Royal College of General Practitioners and accredited by the General Medical Council. The diagnosis and management of arthritic conditions, including the injection of joints and musculo-skeletal medicine, is covered at multiple stages of this programme. In addition all GP Trainees have direct access to the educational resources hosted on the GPNI website. These include webinars and teaching materials relating to the diagnosis and treatment of arthritic conditions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he plans to introduce a single Referral to Treatment measurement for Northern Ireland’s waiting times, similar to other parts of the UK.
answer_text: My Department fully supports the development of a referral to treatment target. This target will require patients to be tracked from when they are referred from their GP and onwards through all subsequent assessment diagnostic and treatment episodes. Such tracking will require a clear and succinct set of rules and well-defined definitions from referral to treatment. From a technical perspective, when the new regional encompass system is fully rolled out next year, Northern Ireland would be well placed to be able to measure the full patient journey.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) for his assessment on the use of the term elective care; and (ii) whether he will consider employing the term planned essential care instead.
answer_text: Elective Care is planned care that is scheduled in advance, such as surgery or outpatient appointments, usually following a referral from a GP or other primary/community health professional. The term Elective Care is a commonly recognised term, widely used by both the HSC, NHS and other healthcare systems. My Department has no plans to change the terminology at this point in time.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he expects to deliver the policy on better communication and support for patients whilst they await treatment.
answer_text: The updated Elective Care Framework (ECF), published in June 2024, recognises the need to support patients who are on waiting lists to better self-manage the condition they have been referred for, as well as their wider health and well-being needs. It sets out the need for development of a policy in this area, which will aim to ensure patients on waiting lists have the best possible outcomes and, through empowerment and support, ensure that their health is optimised and that they are as prepared and as well as possible for their treatment. I intend to publish a waiting well policy in 2025 with rollout immediately thereafter, subject to funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the number of surgical lists orthopaedic surgeons in Northern Ireland undertake; (ii) how this compares with other areas of the UK; and (iii) any discrepancy between the two.
answer_text: On average Northern Ireland orthopaedic surgeons are job planned to deliver at least 10 programme activities (PAs) each week. This consists of 7.5 direct clinical care PAs and 2.5 supporting professional activities which allows for audit and research. The 7.5 direct clinical care PAs are made up of three theatre sessions, two outpatient clinics and 2.5 PAs for ward rounds and patient related administration. This is in line with the Royal College of Surgeons recommendations which are applicable across the UK.The number of direct clinical care PAs will vary depending on frequency of the on-call commitments. Depending on the level of trauma demand, this may mean that consultants undertake four theatre sessions per week. Some Trusts may also fund additional  direct clinical care PAs above the basic 10 PA job plan. It should also be noted in the rest of the UK there is one orthopaedic surgeons per 33,000 people, in Northern Ireland there is one orthopaedic surgeon per 42,000 people.  The British Orthopaedic Associated target is one per 25,000 people.It is accepted that there is an orthopaedic capacity demand gap in Northern Ireland which can only be addressed through the provision of additional recurrent funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether provision exists in Northern Ireland to bring equivalent provisions as in Great Britain in relation to the Bellwin scheme.
answer_text: The Bellwin scheme, which was introduced in 1983, provides emergency financial assistance to local authorities in England. The Scheme of Emergency Financial Assistance, that was introduced by the NI Executive in 2007, has similar arrangements to the Bellwin Scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance, pursuant to AQW 18944/22-27, to detail (i) the Barnett consequentials from the pay increase for NHS workers in Great Britain; and (ii) the proportion of this that went to the Department of Health, similar to what she was able to do in relation to childcare as highlighted in the response to AQW 11019/22-27.
answer_text: ANSWERAs I advised in my response to AQW 18944/22-27, I do not have information on how much of the Barnett consequential received in the Autumn Budget related to Health pay. £609.275 million was received in total, of which the Barnett from Health and Social Care was £333.894 million (55%). DoH was allocated £350m (58%) from the total funding available for allocation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether a trial of giving Bovaer to cattle is taking place in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: A research project, involving a trial of Bovaer in dairy cow diets is being undertaken by the Agri-Food and Biosciences Institute, Hillsborough.  The project is part of the DAERA-directed AFBI research programme.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) the number of (a) sewer points; and (b) storm drains that his Department has permitted since he came to office; and (ii) the location of each of these sewer points and storm drains.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure why street lighting on Markethill Road, Portadown, was removed rather than being replaced, given the accidents that have occurred on this stretch of road.
answer_text: In considering those lighting installations which are deemed to have reached the end of their serviceable life and can no longer be maintained, a survey is carried out of the location and lighting is replaced only in those adopted areas which meet my Department’s policy for the provision of lighting. This can result in the removal and non-replacement of lights in areas such as back pathways, unadopted roads and footways, or rural areas with insufficient density of housing. This policy helps ensure that the limited resources available for the provision of street lighting are directed at those areas where they can provide the maximum benefit to the public, particularly where road or infrastructure safety is concerned.In line with this policy, an assessment of the area in question was undertaken and found that the density of existing development at this location fell short of the stipulated criteria which requires the presence of at least 10 dwellings within a 200m contiguous road length.  A review of PSNI statistics held by my Department has not indicated any instances of night-time collisions involving injury at this location.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i) whether an agreement has been reached which permits vessels from the Republic of Ireland to enter waters around Rockall; and (i) whether Northern Ireland registered vessels can re-enter waters within 6 to 12 miles of the Republic of Ireland.
answer_text: No agreement has been reached between the UK and the Republic of Ireland, or the EU, to permit Irish vessels or any non-UK vessels access to fish in the inshore territorial waters at Rockall.Northern Ireland vessels cannot fish in the Republic of Ireland 6 to 12 nautical mile zone, as this area was not included as part of the access arrangements within the 2021 Trade and Cooperation Agreement between the UK and EU.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance (i) to detail the number of civil servants that have (a) retired; and (b) resigned whist facing disciplinary action in each of the last three years; and (ii) to detail the number of these that were for financial impropriety.
answer_text: ANSWERThere have been 16 cases in the last three financial years where a member of staff resigned during an ongoing discipline case. In the same period there was one case where a staff member retired while an investigation was ongoing. Of these 17 cases in total, one in 2022/23 was potentially due to financial impropriety. However, it must be noted that the investigation was incomplete at the date the officer left the service, therefore any financial wrongdoing had not been established at the date of their leaving. YearResigned with ongoing discipline caseRetired with ongoing discipline case2021/22502022/23602023/2451Total 161

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any plans he has to deal with the large amounts of wastewater which will be generated by the planned gold mine in the Sperrins.
answer_text: The management of public wastewater infrastructure is an operational matter for NI Water. However, I have sought the requested information from NI Water and have been advised as follows:NI Water has advised that it has no wastewater infrastructure in this area and that the planning application for this proposal, LA10/2017/1249/F, indicates that any wastewater generated will be managed by a private treatment system. The responsibility for this system rests with the applicant.  For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact NI Water directly, through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.comMaking direct contact with NI Water will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) to detail the amount of funding his Department has received to manage pressures in relation to asylum seekers in each of the last three years; and (ii) to provide a breakdown of the associated spend.
answer_text: The Department, in conjunction with The Executive Office (TEO), works alongside the Education Authority’s (EA) Intercultural Education Service (IES) to provide funding to support school aged children arriving in Northern Ireland seeking sanctuary from the Asylum community.Over the last 3 financial years, the Department has been provided with funding from Home Office, through TEO’s Full Dispersal Fund to remove barriers to education for such children at the following levels:2022/23 - £915k 2023/24 - £795k (plus an additional £40k from the English Department for Levelling Up Housing & Communities - DLUHC) 2024/25 - £339kThe EA retained around 10% of these funds to employ staff within IES to help manage the pressures with increased numbers of Asylum Seekers requiring assistance, which has allowed it to establish a Hotel Contingency Team within IES who work on a daily basis with families arriving to support them in securing school places, applying for home-to-school transport assistance, uniform grants and free school meals.The EA procured 800 entry level learning devices (£143k) from the larger public sector contract in 2023, to enable families to access out of school hours learning and 500 internet connections where no internet access was available (£180k). This access to learning investment extended across all 3 years.Two ‘welcome hubs’ were established in Malvern Primary School Belfast and Integrated College Glengormley, providing temporary school places for Asylum Seeking pupils in temporary initial hotel accommodation in Greater Belfast. This allowed the schools to employ additional teaching assistants without reducing their core budget.The remainder of the funds were disbursed to schools as additional earmarked funds for the benefit of Asylum-Seeking pupils.  Funds were fully delegated, with schools having the authority to direct their spend to where it was needed the most. The eligible spend items included additional translation and interpreting, additional essential skills including English language development, after school hours study support, temporary transport solutions until bus passes were approved, and emotional health and wellbeing support such as ‘calm boxes’ to help deal with anxiety and trauma post-arrival.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, in light of the information published on the Rainbow Project website in relation to providing advice on chem sex, which involves the use of illegal substances, whether he will review the funding provided by his Department and its arm’s-length bodies to the Rainbow Project.
answer_text: Neither I, nor my Department, carries out an assessment of the content of community/voluntary sector websites. While I do not encourage engaging in any sexually risky behaviour or in any way condone the consumption of illegal drugs for any purpose, I appreciate that there is value in harm reduction advice being provided to individuals who partake in these activities in order to minimise risks and adverse health outcomes. The Rainbow Project has a wide remit, encompassing the wider physical, mental & emotional health needs of LGBTQIA+ communities in Northern Ireland. The organisation provides information and guidance to individuals, taking a harm reduction approach to their work. Through its website, The Rainbow Project acknowledges that many people engage in ‘chem sex’ and other risky behaviours and aims to offer practical advice on how to mitigate the associated health and safety risks for those who do. With the legacy core grant scheme drawing to a close earlier this year, the Department of Health does not currently fund The Rainbow Project directly. The organisation continues to receive funding from the Public Health Agency in respect of key counselling services provided to the LGBTQIA+ community, and the development and provision of information materials is beyond the remit of the service commissioned. All recipients of public funding are subject to accountability measures to ensure that they are delivering on their agreed outcomes and providing value for money.  The Public Health Agency has no plans to review funding for this organisation at this time.

answer_text: Neither I, nor my Department, carries out an assessment of the content of community/voluntary sector websites. answer_text: The Housing Executive has provided the table below which reflects the funding awarded and the funding that was paid out each year for the Rainbow Project:2020/20212021/20222022/20232023/2024££££Funding awarded/ applied for21,12920,94417,27431,401Funding provided by NIHE21,03720,9448,637-8,637Note: In 2022/23, Rainbow was successful in receiving a grant to deliver a five month project from Nov 22 to Mar 23 however,they had to withdraw as they were unable to deliver this project due to staffing challenges. This funding was recouped from Rainbow and is reflected in the table above as a minus figure in 2023/24. No further funding was received in 2023/24.No funding was applied for or allocated in 2019/20 or 2024/25.The Department also awarded a grant of £16,000 to a consortium of organisations which included the Rainbow Project in financial year 2022/23.  The purpose of this funding was to conduct research into conversion practices in Northern Ireland and their impacts.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, pursuant to AQW 16966/22-27, (i) for her assessment of whether the advice on the Rainbow Project website in relation to consent, under the section If your partner is too drunk or too high, is consistent with the position of her Department; and (ii) whether, in light of this advice, she will review the funding provided to the Rainbow Project by her Department and its arm’s-length bodies.
answer_text: I note the advice provided on the Rainbow Project website which highlights capacity (or not) to consent. The law on consent in relation to sexual offending is clearly defined in article 3 of the Sexual Offences (Northern Ireland) Order 2008. It states that "a person consents if he agrees by choice, and has the freedom and capacity to make that choice." As outlined in AQW 16966/22-27, I have no plans to review funding provided to the Rainbow Project by my Department. Any funding provided by the Department’s Non-Departmental Public Bodies is an operational matter for them and you may wish to contact them directly about this.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, pursuant to AQW 16966/22-27, (i) whether she is aware of the information published on the Rainbow Project website in relation to providing advice on chem sex, which involves the use of illegal substances; and (ii) whether she will review the funding provided by her Department and its arm’s-length bodies to the Rainbow Project.
answer_text: I note the advice provided on the Rainbow Project website which highlights that such substances are illegal. The website further offers harm reduction advice which would fall more appropriately to the Department of Health and Public Health Agency for  comment. As detailed in AQW 16966/22-27, I have no plans to review funding provided by my Department to the Rainbow Project. Any funding provided by the Department’s Non-Departmental Public Bodies is an operational matter for them and you may wish to contact them directly about this.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline any research that was undertaken on the impact of closing the Boyne Bridge for a year on traders in the Sandy Row and Grosvenor Road areas.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the proposed 400-space park and ride facility and upgrades at Ballymena Bus and Train Station.
answer_text: Subject to the approval of the full business case and availability of funding, construction of the new Park and Ride facility is scheduled to commence in Quarter 3 2025 and will be operational a year later.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the amount of money that has been saved on electricity by the Causeway Hospital since the solar panels were installed; and (ii) the cost of installing the solar panels.
answer_text: The Causeway Hospital Solar PV system became operational in April 2025.  The amount of money saved to date on electricity between April and November 2025 is circa £113,000.00.The cost to install and commission the solar panels was £1,214,522.00.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail (i) the Barnett consequentials from the pay increase for NHS workers in Great Britain; and (ii) the proportion of this that went to the Department of Health.
answer_text: The total Barnett received in the Autumn Budget was £609.275 million of which the Barnett from Health and Social Care was £333.894 million (55%). I do not have any further breakdown of what element of this amount related to pay. Allocations received through Barnett consequentials are unhypothecated meaning that the Executive can determine allocations for specific priorities and programmes regardless of the nature of the comparable spending that gave rise to the Barnett allocation. Department of Health was allocated £350m (58%) from the total funding available for allocation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of children referred to counselling without their parents knowledge in each of the last three years, broken down by local council area.
answer_text: The Department of Education does not hold this information.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission, pursuant to AQW 17213/22-27, whether it will put these items on public display.
answer_text: The Assembly Commission held a temporary exhibition in September 2018 when many of the items listed in AQW 17213/22-27 were available for public viewing.  Then in 2021, the Assembly Commission agreed to hold a review of the items on display in Parliament Buildings.  This review was informed by historical advice from Dr Éamon Phoenix and considered all of the items then held in storage by the Assembly Commission for their historical value and relevance to the history of the building.  The Assembly Commission completed the review of the items on display in Parliament Buildings in 2022 and unveiled a revised collection of items and images which depicted key figures and events connected to the parliamentary history of Parliament Buildings.   As is common in historic buildings, there is limited public display space available to exhibit every item in an organised and coherent fashion.  Therefore, the portraits and items which were considered of most historical value and relevance to the building were removed from storage and included in the new display.  As the 2021 review was a significant piece of work, informed by external expertise, the Assembly Commission remains content to respect the outcomes of the review, particularly given the positive feedback from visitors.  Those items which remain in storage were considered to be of limited historical value or of more limited relevance to the building. However, the Assembly Commission agreed that it was open to the possibility of loaning these items for display to relevant organisations and buildings which could display them in appropriate conditions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of children being referred to counselling without their parents' knowledge.
answer_text: The current post-primary counselling service, HOPE Service, is managed by the Education Authority.The HOPE Service Handbook for Providers and Educational Settings is available on the EA website and states that pupils can choose to engage with counselling without parental consent, if deemed to be ‘Gillick competent’.  The Handbook outlines the referral process.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the annual taxi bill for (i) his Department; and (ii) its arm's-length bodies, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The annual taxi expenditure for (i) my Department and (ii) the Department’s Arm's-Length Bodies, in each of the last five years, is set out in the tables below.Department of Health2019/20£k2020/21£k2021/22£k2022/23£k2023/24£k7.70.70.31.52.1Arms-Length Bodies2019/20£m2020/21£m2021/22£m2022/23£m2023/24£m7681111The use of taxis by Health and Social Care Trusts is planned and monitored and taxis are only used when it is not economically viable to use Trust transport for vulnerable patients and clients, particularly children in care and adults with a physical disability or learning disability. transport  There has been a notable increase in spend on public taxis over the past two years by the Northern Ireland Ambulance Service (NIAS) due largely to reduced capacity of the Voluntary Car Service (VCS), with a significant loss of Voluntary Car Service (VCS) capacity throughout the COVID Pandemic as not all Volunteer Drivers which were stood down at the outset, returned to the service.  NIAS is running an ongoing VCS recruitment campaign to improve the position.The continued HSC system wide pressures and the associated deteriorating position with Ambulance Handover Delays at Emergency Departments has also resulted in a significant loss of operational capacity to respond to emergency calls. This has increased the reliance on taxis for non-emergency work in the latter two years. The Trust is continuing with plans to reduce the use of this modality of conveyance and is engaged with DoH colleagues regarding the DoH development of eligibility criteria for non-emergency transport,Taxi use by NIBTS is for the transportation of blood products, blood- donors and staff.NIFRS does not have an account with any taxi firms and rarely utilises taxi services.

answer_text: The Minister of Health has never met the Chairperson of the Committee for Health to discuss questions to be asked in an evidence session with the Committee.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what steps he has taken to roll-back on transgender ideology in schools.
answer_text: My department does not promote, issue guidance on, or provide funding in respect of transgender ideology in schools in Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the number of asylum seekers in Northern Ireland currently in hotel accommodation, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: The provision of accommodation for asylum seekers is not devolved, and as such, The Executive Office has no remit in this. The Home Office published data indicates that as of the end of June 2024 there were 301 asylum seekers housed in hotel style accommodation here.  These statistics are broken down by relevant Council area, rather than constituencies:Council Asylum seekers in hotels Antrim and Newtownabbey53Ards and North Down28Belfast149Causeway Coast and Glens43Derry City and Strabane28Total 301This dataset can be found on the Home Office website at Immigration system statistics data tables - GOV.UK. This data is updated and published quarterly.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any dates on which she met the Chairperson of the Committee for Finance to discuss questions to be asked in an evidence session with the Committee.
answer_text: To date, the Finance Minister has not met with the Chairperson of the Committee for Finance in advance of Committee meetings but has on occasion met with the Chair and/or deputy Chair to discuss emerging issues.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any dates on which she met the Chairperson of the Committee for Justice to discuss questions to be asked in an evidence session with the Committee.
answer_text: Since the return of the Assembly, I have met with the Chair and Deputy Chair of the Justice Committee on 19 February 2024, 22 April 2024 and 4 November 2024 to discuss ongoing issues including the Department’s legislative programme, the Committee’s forward work programme and opportunities for collaborative working.  I often meet with the Chair and Deputy Chair in advance of appearing before the Committee to discuss the intended focus of the evidence session to ensure that it is productive for me and Committee members.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any dates on which he met the Chairperson of the Committee for the Economy to discuss questions to be asked in an evidence session with the Committee.
answer_text: To date, I have not met with the Chairperson of the Committee in advance of Committee meetings.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any dates on which he met the Chairperson of the Committee for Infrastructure to discuss questions to be asked in an evidence session with the Committee.
answer_text: I have not met with the Chair of the Committee for Infrastructure to discuss questions to be asked in an evidence session with the Committee.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount that needs to be invested before his Department will move from a policy of a limited service policy in each division.
answer_text: The level of repairs that can be carried out in any financial year depends on the funding allocated and until such times as sufficient funding is made available, it will not be possible to change the current level of service. It is estimated that c.£60m would be required for my Department to deliver normal Essential Maintenance service levels.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any dates on which he met the Chairperson of the Committee for Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to discuss questions to be asked in an evidence session with the Committee.
answer_text: From 3 February 2024 when I was appointed as Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs I have met the Chair and Deputy Chair of the Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs Committee on a number of occasions, including: 20 February, 5 March, 2 July, 30 July, 3 October, 22 October and 19 November.  These meetings were to both discuss current and future issues and, on relevant occasions, to provide a briefing and answer any relevant questions in advance of Oral Statements.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any dates on which he met the Chairperson of the Committee for Education to discuss questions to be asked in an evidence session with the Committee.
answer_text: I appeared before the Committee for Education on the 20th March and 12th June 2024. No meetings were scheduled with the Chairperson of the Committee for Education prior to my attendance on either date.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any dates on which (i) the First Minister or deputy First Minister; and (ii) either of the junior Ministers met the Chairperson of the Committee for the Executive Office to discuss questions to be asked in an evidence session with the Committee.
answer_text: The Member will already be aware that the First Minister met the Chairperson of the Committee for the Executive Office on 23 October to discuss her scheduled evidence session.  At that meeting the First Minister and Chairperson discussed a letter which had previously been sent from the Committee to the Executive Office advising that it had agreed to share questions in advance and listing several questions submitted by Members. Junior Ministers have not met the Chairperson for the purpose stated.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the rationale for approving the appointment of Patricia MacBride to the Northern Ireland Judicial Appointments Commission, in light of reports of how she previously described her brother as an IRA volunteer who was killed on active service in 1984.
answer_text: The Justice (Northern Ireland) Act 2002 outlines the arrangements for appointment of members to the NI Judicial Appointments Commission.These arrangements include that five members, nominated by the Lord/Lady Chief Justice, are to be appointed as judicial members of the commission.Ms MacBride was nominated by the Lady Chief Justice to serve as a judicial member of the Commission and was appointed on this basis. Ms MacBride has served a lay magistrate since 2005.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister who owns the property in which the North South Ministerial Council is located.
answer_text: The NSMC Joint Secretariat building is owned by Armagh City, Banbridge and Craigavon Borough Council.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he will make urgent representations to the Ulster Museum in relation to the Oughley Shed exhibit, in light of concerns of parents of young children about its contents.
answer_text: This exhibit is no longer on display at the Ulster Museum. I believe museums should be safe, inclusive spaces and whilst the matter of exhibitions at the Ulster Museum is an operational issue for National Museums NI, my department received assurances that the Ulster Museum put in place safeguards at this exhibit to ensure that young children did not access any material that is not age appropriate.

answer_text: The Executive Office does not operate a Windsor Framework funding website.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail what the MLKDC - revaluation (340)K, referenced in the October Monitoring Round papers provided to the Committee for the Executive Office, was spent on.
answer_text: The 340k is a reduction in the amount of Annually Managed Expenditure (AME) allocated to MLKDC from 1,000k to 660k.  This adjustment is the reprofiling of the AME requirement within the current financial year due to a change in the projected completion date of capital works to the WW2 hangars. The AME budget provides cover for any movement in valuation of MLKDC’s assets at year end.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any plans he has to lower the age of testing for prostate cancer.
answer_text: Screening for prostate cancer is currently not recommended by the UK National Screening Committee (UK NSC). They last considered the evidence for screening for prostate cancer in 2020 and concluded that screening was not recommended.  This is because of the poor ability of the prostate specific antigen (PSA) test to predict cancer, which could lead to unnecessary tests that carry risks of potentially life-changing harm. Additionally, some prostate cancers may not produce elevated PSA levels, leading to false-negative results that provide false reassurance. Prostate cancer is rare in men under 50 but the risk increases with age. The UK NSC has commissioned a prostate cancer screening evidence review and cost effectiveness model, and it is expected to review this work during 2025. The UK NSC process will include a public consultation. The UK Health Departments, through the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR), have invested in a significant programme of research to address the evidence gaps in this important area. A clinical trial (called the TRANSFORM trial), which will compare the most promising tests and provide evidence about the best way to screen for prostate cancer, is due to start in early 2025.My department will continue to be guided by the UK NSC’s recommendations on proposals for screening programmes. Anyone with symptoms which they believe may be related to the prostate gland should contact their GP.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether she will use the Barnett consequential of the 40 per cent rates relief scheme for independent retailers and other high street businesses in England to provide appropriate rates relief for high street businesses in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I refer the member to my answer to AQW 17794/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of people that have been fined for vaping on Translink (i) buses; and (ii) trains in each of the last three years.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and can confirm that there have not been any fines for vaping on board buses or trains in the last three years. I would highlight that the byelaw was repealed when the smoking ban came in and Translink can only issue fines for vaping or smoking on a bus with local councils being responsible for issuing fines on buses. For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk. Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he expects a supply of insulin pumps and hybrid closed loop systems to be issued to people living with type 1 diabetes.
answer_text: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence TA943, Hybrid closed loop systems for managing blood glucose levels in Type 1 diabetes, has been endorsed as applicable in Northern Ireland. Subsequently, my Department has requested work to be undertaken to understand the implications of this decision.  Overall provision; priority groups; system costs; workforce and communication requirements to both enable and support, are being considered. Given the scale of investment needed, financial requirements are also being considered, as funding is required for devices as well as resources to upskill and recruit the workforce needed to offer Hybrid Closed Loop safely, effectively and equitably across the region.  Final decision-making and commissioning arrangements for NI will be made, supported by an implementation plan. This will support the integration continuous glucose monitoring technologies into diabetes care and will be communicated to all stakeholders.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any measures he is taking to ensure that people living with diabetes have regular checks to avoid issues arising from poor management of the disease.
answer_text: Diabetes affects nearly 112,000 people in NI, with the number growing. Services have been recovering post covid, with additional investment to reduce waiting lists. Delivery of care is provided by diabetes specialist multidisciplinary teams, with patients in higher risk groups prioritised for review first. However, waiting lists remain long, with limited specialist workforce.An integrated response is required for an increasing population presenting with multi-morbidities, utilising all existing assets across workforce, infrastructure, technology, finance and Community Voluntary Sector.Working with key stakeholders, a Regional Integrated Pathway for Diabetes has been defined over the last two years.  This Regional Pathway has been developed in line with the Diabetes Strategic Framework (2016).  It will standardise access and provide equity across Northern Ireland, through the integration of diabetes services, supporting treatment and care now and in the future.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any plans he has to undertake a clinical audit to measure the effectiveness of diabetes care in Northern Ireland, similar to the Diabetes Audit used in England and Wales.
answer_text: The Diabetes Strategic Framework (2016) included an action to participate in National Diabetes Audit, similar to the Diabetes Audit used in England and Wales.The Department of Health remains committed to working towards participation in the National Diabetes Audit.  However, there have been significant challenges in progressing this recommendation, as the required infrastructure is not currently in place to participate fully in the audit.With the full implementation of encompass, currently being rolled out across all Health and Social Care Trusts, the provision of data that is both available and extractable for the region will be available.  This will support participation in the National Diabetes Audit in the future.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 17455/22-27, to detail the longest time anyone has waited for a (i) hip replacement; and (ii) knee replacement in the (a) Belfast Health and Social Care Trust; (b) Southern Health and Social Care Trust; and (c) Western Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The following table outlines the current longest waiting patient for both a hip replacement and knee replacement in Belfast, Southern and Western Health & Social Care Trusts as at November 2024.Longest waiting patient in weeksTrustHipsKneesBelfast379 407 Southern422 427Western445 431The current waiting times for primary joint replacement are unacceptable. question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission to detail the hours worked by those employed in Hansard's Interpretation and Translation Service.
answer_text: The standard working week for all staff employed by the Assembly Commission is 37 hours, (excluding meal breaks). Working hours are dictated by the mode of operation of the Assembly.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission to detail the total cost to date of Hansard's Interpretation and Translation Service.
answer_text: On 15 June 2021 the Assembly directed the Assembly Commission: “where there is appropriate demand and subject to review after six months, to provide a simultaneous and passive system for interpretation in the Assembly.”The Interpretation and Translation Unit (‘the Unit’) has been operational and available to interpret from Irish to English since May 2022. During the period from May 2022 to January 2024, when the Assembly was not carrying out its full programme of normal plenary and committee business, the total cost of the Unit was £287,274.Following the resumption of normal Assembly business on 3 February 2024, the cost of the Unit to date has been £150,672.The planned review of the interpretation service was undertaken by the Assembly Commission in October 2024 i.e. six ‘sitting’ months following the resumption of Assembly business in February 2024. On the basis of the established demand for the interpretation of Irish, there were no grounds to justify increasing the level of service, nor was there any scope to reduce the level of service without affecting the ability of those Members who wish to speak Irish to avail of the specific provision set out in 'New Decade, New Approach' and cited in the resolution of the Assembly.Following the October 2024 review of the interpretation service, permanent recruitment to the interpretation roles will commence, and will be based on job descriptions to allow for the appointment of full-time, hybrid English/Irish parliamentary reporters/interpreters. Whilst the primary role of these staff will be to interpret from Irish to English on demand, they will also be available to produce Official Reports in English when interpretation is not required. In addition, the Unitwill support the Assembly Commission in its compliance with the forthcoming standards of best practice relating to the use of the Irish language by public authorities.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, in light of the Strategic Framework to End Violence Against Women and Girls, for their assessment of biological men gaining access to female-only spaces.
answer_text: The Ending Violence Against Women and Girls Strategic Framework contains a key outcome that women and girls are safe and feel safe everywhere.The provision of single sex spaces is provided for by legislation.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, given their Department's responsibility for representing the interests of victims, why they did not issue a statement of condemnation following pro-IRA shouting at a memorial event to mark the 1987 Remembrance Day bombing in Enniskillen.
answer_text: All expressions of sectarianism are wrong. The victims of the 1987 Remembrance Day bombing must be allowed to commemorate the event with the dignity and respect they deserve.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 17214/22-27, if it is the case that pronouns only to be used to school is still listed in the intranet system for Northern Ireland’s schools.
answer_text: Schools in Northern Ireland, like other regions in the UK, use the ESS School Information Management System (SIMS) which provides schools with the option to add personal pronouns to school records. The inclusion of personal pronouns in school records is not mandatory and each school has the autonomy to decide whether or not to use this feature.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, in light of Ards and North Down Borough Council not publishing the names of councillors who attempted to call in the decision to fly the Union Flag from war memorials in the borough, to detail any measures he will take to promote transparency in local government.
answer_text: I am considering a review of the local government legislative framework. Transparency of local government may form part of that review.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail all organisations that have received funding from his Department or its arms-length bodies for the delivery of Relationships and Sexuality Education since he came to office.
answer_text: I can confirm that neither my Department nor any of its arms-length bodies have provided funding to organisations in respect of the delivery of Relationships and Sexuality Education since I became Minister in February 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 17214/22-27, to detail any efforts he has made to remove this guidance, including any relevant dates.
answer_text: As noted in my previous response, my department does not offer guidance in this regard. Guidance for Schools, EOTAS Centres and Youth Service on Supporting Transgender Young People is a matter for the Education Authority.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 17215/22-27, to detail any efforts he intends to take to collect this data.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 8633/22-27, (i) when the meeting (a) was requested; and (b) took place; and (ii) to detail the outcome of the meeting.
answer_text: I requested a meeting with the Libraries NI Chief Executive and Chairperson of the Board on 25 April 2024. Due to existing diary commitments, the meeting took place on 18 June 2024.Whilst the matter of book stock is an operational issue for Libraries NI, I reiterated my view that books should be age appropriate and for libraries to be a safe place for both parents and children.  The Chair and Chief Executive outlined to me the established procedures in place to manage book stock and to deal with concerns or complaints about specific books. I would encourage parents who have concerns to utilise that procedure and make their views known. I recognise the important role libraries play in communities across Northern Ireland. This is an issue that I will continue to monitor.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 15146/22-27, whether they will commit to providing regular updates to the Assembly on the work of the new working group.
answer_text: The Working Group meets at an official level only and is intended to supplement the existing formal role for the Executive as part of the UK delegation for Windsor Framework governance bodies. Queries about specific elements of the implementation of the Windsor Framework should be addressed to the relevant Executive department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what representations he plans to make to His Majesty’s Government to oppose its plan to apply an inheritance tax on family farms worth over £1 million.
answer_text: The recent announcement in the Budget regarding the limitation of agricultural property relief and business property relief at a rate of 100% to a combined value of £1m has caused great concern in the agricultural community.  I met with the NI Secretary of State on 4th November 2024 and expressed my concerns and urged that the matter be reconsidered. I have asked him to engage with the farming community to respond to the concerns being raised.My Department has carried out some initial anlysis of the potential impact of the announcements on our local farming community, the detail of which is available at the NI Assembly Library (https://www.niassembly.gov.uk/assembly-business/research-and-information-service-raise/deposited-papers-2024/).  I have engaged with the Finance Minister on the matter and shared this analysis with Department of Finance officials given that it is a fiscal measure.I will continue to do all that I can to support the farming industry in Northern Ireland on this issue within the remit of DAERA.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any reported water pollution incidents from Randox Laboratories in 2023.
answer_text: Randox Laboratories has seven sites across Northern Ireland. NIEA has no record of reported or confirmed water pollution incidents in connection with any of these sites in 2023.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any plans they have to establish investigations into child abuse which fall outside the scope of the Historical Institutional Abuse inquiry.
answer_text: In 2016, the Executive agreed to establish an independently chaired Inter-Departmental Working Group (IDWG) to take forward work on historic Mother and Baby Homes, Magdalene Laundries and historical clerical child abuse (HCCA) which fell outside the terms of reference of the Historical Institutional Abuse Inquiry.The research element of Mother and Baby Homes and Magdalene Laundries was completed following the publication of the research report in January 2021 and the Executive’s agreement in November 2021 to implement a Design Panel’s recommendations in full.The first stage of the investigation began with the appointment of an Independent Panel in April 2023, which is currently taking testimony from those affected.  A public consultation on the statutory public inquiry and redress scheme closed in September 2024. The next key stage will be to bring forward legislation.The IDWG continues to work on investigation into and development of recommendations on HCCA. The IDWG has commissioned research to examine the extent and systemic nature of HCCA here. The purpose of the research work is to support the IDWG to provide informed, evidence-based recommendations to Ministers. Work is progressing on three separate pieces of research:A.	A review of what records are held within the faith sector and by criminal justice agencies and social services, to understand how they responded to historical allegations of clerical abuse within the faith sector. B.	A review of victims’ and survivors’ experience and expectations. And  C.	A review of current safeguarding practices within the faith sector.We look forward to the conclusion of the research projects in Spring next year, and subsequently receiving advice and recommendations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the volume of sewage and household waste deposited in Lough Neagh in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Environment Agency (NIEA) does not hold any information on the volume of sewage and household waste deposited into Lough Neagh in each of the past 5 years. NIEA regulates sewage discharges to the water environment under the Water Order (as amended) 1999 whereby Water Order Discharge Consents are issued to single dwellings, private sewage infrastructure serving residential / commercial properties in addition to NI Water. These Consents require authorised dischargers to treat sewage to a level that can be sustained by the receiving environment, and to maintain their treatments to avoid pollution.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any reports of major pollution incidents in the Sixmilewater between January 2023 and July 2023.
answer_text: Pollution incidents reported to the Northern Ireland Environment Agency are classified as High, Medium and Low severity. Of the substantiated reports received in the inclusive period January to July 2023, none were classified as high severity. There were two medium severity, and 14 low severity water pollution incidents reported within the Six Mile Water River catchment in this time period.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 16791/22-27, when he will implement measures to capture this data, in order to increase public confidence.
answer_text: NIAS are now looking at options to capture this information going forward and will advise if this is feasible with their current systems.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission whether it applied for a portrait of His Majesty the King.
answer_text: The Assembly Commission has considered initial advice from officials, including equality screening, in relation to a request to display a portrait of King Charles III.  Discussions with officials to establish if a consensus could be reached on this matter have not yet concluded.  As the Assembly Commission is well used to producing images for display within its wider collection on the walls of Parliament Buildings, it was agreed that the Assembly Commission did not need to rely on the Cabinet Office scheme to secure a portrait for display if the request was agreed.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission whether the examination into matters relating to Michael McMonagle will seek to establish how many days a week he worked in Michelle O'Neill's constituency office.
answer_text: I refer the Member to AQW 16723/22-27 on 17 October 2024 which set out the approach being taken to the examination of these matters.  This included that the matter is being examined under the authority of the Clerk/ Chief Executive as the Assembly Commission’s Accounting Officer.  Assembly Commission Members have no role in this process and therefore are not being updated with the detail of its operation.The Assembly Commission has set out that it takes matters relating to the administration of public funds extremely seriously and that it is important that these matters are dealt with in a robust way which follows due process and examines the evidence.  The examination of potential issues arising in relation to the employment of Mr McMonagle is being undertaken to ensure that there has been compliance with the Assembly Members’ (Salaries and Expenses) Determination 2016.  Therefore, matters relevant to establishing compliance with the determination will be examined.However, the Assembly Commission has also been clear that it will not be providing a running commentary on the detail of this process while it is continuing.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether there are plans to reinstate the cross border arrangements for (i) hip replacements; and (ii) knee replacements in each Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The Republic of Ireland Reimbursement Scheme (ROIRS) closed to new applications on 21 September 2022 when all funding resources had been exhausted. The funding available this year is significantly less than what is required to reduce waiting lists and, therefore, our focus is on supporting those on suspect cancer and time critical waiting lists.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission whether the detailed examination into the pay arrangements pertaining to Michael McMonagle will engage with his former employers.
answer_text: I refer the Member to AQW 16723/22-27 on 17 October 2024 which set out the approach being taken to the examination of these matters.  This included that the matter is being examined under the authority of the Clerk/ Chief Executive as the Assembly Commission’s Accounting Officer.  Assembly Commission Members have no role in this process and therefore are not being updated with the detail of its operation.The Assembly Commission has set out that it takes matters relating to the administration of public funds extremely seriously and that it is important that these matters are dealt with in a robust way which follows due process and examines the evidence.  The examination of potential issues arising in relation to the employment of Mr McMonagle is being undertaken to ensure that there has been compliance with the Assembly Members’ (Salaries and Expenses) Determination 2016.  When reviewing concerns around the compliance of expenditure with the determination, it is normal that officials would engage with the relevant Member or party.However, the Assembly Commission has also been clear that it will not be providing a running commentary on the detail of this process while it is continuing.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether the deputy First Minister agreed to the First Minister meeting with the Chairperson of the Committee for the Executive Office in advance of the Committee meeting on 23 October 2024.
To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the number of times, on foot of the right to be forgotten, that references to individuals have been removed from court judgements in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), which ordinarily provides individuals with rights concerning their personal information, does not apply where personal data is processed by the judiciary exercising judicial functions.The removal of references to individuals within court judgments is a matter for the independent judiciary.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 15146/22-27, who represents the Executive Office on the new working group.
answer_text: Senior officials from the Intergovernmental and International Directorate in The Executive Office attend the working group.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) how; and (ii) when he raised the issue of multi-year investment in thrombectomy with the Executive.
answer_text: I have not raised the issue of multi-year investment in thrombectomy directly with the Executive. However, my Department has been engaging with the Department of Finance on this issue. My Department submitted details of additional requirements, including bids in relation to thrombectomy, to the Department of Finance as part of the Budget 2024-25 Process: Information Gathering. The Resource budget available to Health was insufficient to cover the significant increases in costs we are facing in 2024-25 as a result of pay and price inflation and rising demand. Consequently, there has been insufficient funding to meet all of our pressures. The Department’s plan to achieve breakeven is dependent on challenging savings targets being delivered and anticipated income being received.My Department has also submitted details of additional funding requirements for thrombectomy for future years to the Department of Finance as part of the Budget 2025-26 Process: Information Gathering. This information was submitted to the Department of Finance on 9th October 2024 and will inform the Executive’s consideration of the budget for the three years 2025-28.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of whether there is a conflict of interest in a university employing a former Minister, who had the policy remit for higher education, without a public recruitment process.

To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether the deputy First Minister agreed to commission legal advice on any aspect of the First Minister’s appearance at the Committee for the Executive Office on 23 October 2024; (ii) whether that advice was sought internally via the Departmental Solicitors Office or externally; and (iii) what were the questions upon which advice was sought.
answer_text: Officials commissioned legal advice from the Departmental Solicitors Office to inform its briefing to Ministers.  Upon receipt, the advice was shared with both Ministers. The nature and substance of this advice is protected by legal professional privilege.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether the deputy First Minister agreed to the First Minister attending the Committee for the Executive Office meeting by herself on 23 October 2024.
answer_text: The First Minister and deputy First Minister act jointly in the exercise of their statutory responsibilities.  The First Minister’s appearance at the meeting of the Committee for the Executive Office on 23 October 2024 was with the consent of both Ministers.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 15146/22-27, to detail the dates on which the new working group has met.
answer_text: There are a range of contacts between Northern Ireland Executive and UK Government officials on the operation of the Windsor Framework. the working group outlined in the Safeguarding the Union Command Paper, led by senior officials from both sides, has already met to discuss its working approach and will meet again formally in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of nurses with expertise in stroke care in each of the last five years, broken down by hospital site.
answer_text: There are a range of nursing roles involved in providing stroke care, with the level of specialism varying by role and hospital site.My Department intends to undertake further research on these roles as part of the commitment set out in the Stroke Action Plan to take forward a stroke workforce review. Planning for the workforce review is underway with a view to commencing the review in 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he will commit to a 24/7 thrombectomy service in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Expanding the regional thrombectomy service to 24/7 in Northern Ireland remains one of my key priorities.This will require significant investment including the recruitment of specialist posts within a limited workforce pool. Given the budget, we are not yet in a position to move to a 24/7 service. However, I am committed to ensuring as many people as possible from across Northern Ireland continue to benefit from the service within the current hours of operation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether the Health and Social Care Pension Schemes (Partial Retirement etc.) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2024 are operational and fully available to all relevant employees.
answer_text: The Department introduced legislation, in April this year, providing the legal framework for the introduction of pension flexibilities for the 1995 Section of the HSC Pension Scheme.  Employers and HSC Pension Service are working to put the necessary application processes in place with the hope of having it available to all relevant members as soon as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of abortions that have been carried out since the change in legislation, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The Department publishes an annual report on abortions carried out in Northern Ireland which is broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.The most recent publication on Northern Ireland abortion data was released on 30 January 2025 and can be accessed at the following link: https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/publications/northern-ireland-abortion-statistics-202324 .

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the hospitals that have stroke physicians.
answer_text: Acute stroke care is provided by Consultants at the following hospitals: Royal Victoria, Antrim Area, Causeway, Ulster, Craigavon, Daisy Hill, South West Acute and Altnagelvin.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the current advice provided to schools on using pronouns which are not the same as a pupil's biological sex without informing the pupil's parent or guardian.
answer_text: The Department of Education does not offer guidance in this regard, however the Education Authority provides non-statutory advice in its Guidance for Schools, EOTAS Centres and Youth Service on Supporting Transgender Young People.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) whether Article 26 of the Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 applies to Boards of Governors of grant-aided schools; and (ii) to detail any statutory provision that bestows similar obligations on a (a) school; and (b) Board of Governors.
answer_text: Article 26 (General duty of authority) of the Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 does not apply to Boards of Governors of grant-aided schools.  Boards of Governors of grant-aided schools have statutory duties under Articles 17 and 18 of the Education and Libraries (Northern Ireland) Order 2003 to promote and safeguard the welfare of all of their pupils.The Department of Education statutory guidance ‘Safeguarding and Child Protection – a Guide for Schools’ is kept updated regularly and it provides detailed advice and guidance for schools in relation to their responsibilities for child protection and procedures to be followed. The guidance operates along with other safeguarding policies, for example in relation to online safety, on bullying and on promoting positive behaviour.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the percentage of prisoners who are foreign nationals, broken down by prison facility.
answer_text: The percentage of prisoners who are foreign nationals broken down by prison facility are set out in the table below:-Prison EstablishmentPercentage of Foreign NationalsMaghaberry11.2%Magilligan3.6%Hydebank Wood12.0%Total9.3%Notes:Data is unpublished management information;Data is a snapshot information taken at midnight on 22nd October 2024;Foreign Nationals are based on those with a Foreign National marker on the Prison Records Information System;Nationality is self-declared by prisoners on arrival at a prison and these declarations are not investigated or verified by prison staff;Determining the actual nationality of a prisoner is a matter for the Home Office, andRecorded nationality may change during a prisoner's time in custody if they make further self-declarations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of schools in the (i) controlled; (ii) maintained; (iii) integrated; and (iv) Irish medium sector that have adopted a gender-neutral approach to (a) toilet facilities; and (b) changing facilities.
answer_text: My department does not hold information regarding the number of schools in Northern Ireland that have adopted a gender-neutral approach to toilet facilities and changing facilities. The management and availability of these facilities is an operational responsibility for school management.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission to detail all items of historical importance it currently holds in storage.
answer_text: The Assembly Commission completed a review of the items on display in Parliament Buildings in 2022 and installed a new collection of items and images which depicted key figures and events connected to the parliamentary history of the building.  This review was informed by historical advice from Dr Éamon Phoenix.  At that time, the portraits and items which the Assembly Commission held in storage, but which were considered of most historical value and relevance to the parliamentary history of the building were removed from storage and included in the new collection of items on display.A variety of items remain in storage (Annex 1), but these were considered to be of more limited relevance to the parliamentary history of the building or of limited historical value.
